definition.en: Soul is the life within everything that is.
definition.zh: 灵魂是万有之内的生命。

definition.en: The soul or entity is itself the most highly motivated, most highly energized, and most potent consciousness-unit known in any universe.
definition.zh: 灵魂或存有本身就是任何宇宙中所知的最具动机、最充满能量、最强大的意识单位。

definition.en: Soul or entity, then, is more than consciousness.
definition.zh: 因此，灵魂或存有超越了意识。

definition.en: Yet the soul is above all a fountain of energy, creativity, and action that shows its characteristics in life precisely through the ever-changing emotions.
definition.zh: 然而，灵魂首先是能量、创造力与行动的源泉，它正是通过不断变化的情绪在生命中展现其特性。

definition.en: Souls are also creative psychic structures, ever-changing and yet always retaining individual integrity, and all are dependent one upon the other.
definition.zh: 灵魂也是具有创造性的心灵结构，不断变化却始终保持着个体的完整性，并且彼此相互依存。

explanation: 灵魂是存在于一切事物中的生命力，而非某个物体或永恒化的自我。它不是你可以拥有或失去的东西，也不是你死后才获得的奖赏。灵魂是高度创造性的能量，是宇宙中最有动力、最强大的意识单元。你的意识只是灵魂使用的一种工具，就像眼睛用于看东西一样——你并非你的意识，意识是你可用的属性。灵魂包含你当前的人格，但远不止于此：它还包括你的转世人格、可能自我，以及你无法想象的个性潜能。灵魂通过不断变化的情绪展现其特性，它既是能量的源泉，也是创造与行动的本源。生物性即是灵性，就像花朵从泥土的粪便中生长一样，灵魂与物质世界并非对立。你无需寻找所谓的“灵魂伴侣”来完整自己，因为你从来不是半个灵魂。

quotes.text: But all things have consciousness, and in those terms possess a soul-nature. There are no gradations as to soul. Soul is the life within everything that is. Of course the fetus "has a soul" -- but in the same way, if you think in those terms, then each cell within the fetus must be granted a soul (leaning forward with humorous emphasis, voice deeper). The course of a cell is not predetermined. Cells are usually very cooperative, particularly as they form the structures of the body.3
quotes.translation: 然而万物皆有意识，就这些术语而言，万物都拥有灵魂本质。灵魂没有等级之分。灵魂是一切存在之物内在的生命。当然，胎儿"拥有灵魂"——但同理，如果你用这些术语思考，那么胎儿体内的每个细胞都必须被赋予灵魂（身体前倾，带着幽默的强调，声音低沉）。细胞的历程并非预先注定。细胞通常非常合作，尤其在它们构成身体结构时。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: People have written here asking about soul mates.3 In certain circles this is the latest vogue. The idea is an old one; it is based upon the reality of counterparts, and presents another version of the theory. But, again, it is treated with an almost pompous seriousness. (Pause.) Many of those who use the term do it to hide rather than to release their own joyful abilities. They spend time searching for their soul mates -- but the search involves them in a pilgrimage for a kind of impossible communication with another, in which all division is lost, with the two then trying to join in a cementing oneness, suffocating all sense of play or creativity. You are not one part, or one half, of another soul,4 searching through the annals of time for your partner, undone until you are completed by your soul mate.
quotes.translation: 有人在此写信询问灵魂伴侣。在某些圈子里，这是最新的时尚。这个想法由来已久；它基于对应体的现实，并呈现了该理论的另一个版本。但同样，它被以一种近乎浮夸的严肃态度对待。（停顿。）许多使用这个术语的人，是为了隐藏而非释放自己喜悦的能力。他们花费时间寻找灵魂伴侣——但这种寻找让他们踏上了一场朝圣之旅，寻求与另一个人达成某种不可能的沟通，在这种沟通中，所有分裂都消失，两人试图融合成一种固化的合一，扼杀所有游戏或创造的感觉。你不是另一个灵魂的一部分或一半，在时间的编年史中寻找你的伴侣，在未得到灵魂伴侣的补全之前，你是不完整的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Many individuals imagine the soul to be an immortalized ego, forgetting that the ego as you know it is only a small portion of the self; so this section of the personality is simply projected onward, ad infinitum, so to speak. Because the dimensions of your reality are so little understood, your concepts are bound to be limited. In considering "immortality," mankind seems to hope for further egotistical development, and yet he objects to the idea that such development might involve change. He says through his religions that he has a soul indeed, without even asking what a soul is, and often he seems to regard it, again, as an object in his possession.
quotes.translation: 许多人想象灵魂是一个不朽的自我，忘记了你们所知的自我只是自我的一小部分；所以人格的这一部分只是被无限地投射出去，可以这么说。因为你们现实的维度如此不被理解，你们的概念必然受限。在考虑"不朽"时，人类似乎希望进一步自我发展，然而他又反对这种发展可能涉及改变的想法。他通过他的宗教说他确实拥有灵魂，却甚至不问灵魂是什么，而且他常常再次将其视为他所拥有的一件物品。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In many philosophies this sort of idea is retained -- the soul being returned to a primal giver, or being dissolved in a nebulous state somewhere between being and nonbeing. The soul is, however, first of all creative. It can be discussed from many viewpoints. Its characteristics can be given to some degree, and indeed most of my readers could find out these characteristics for themselves if they were highly enough motivated, and if this was their main concern. The soul or entity is itself the most highly motivated, most highly energized, and most potent consciousness-unit known in any universe.
quotes.translation: 在许多哲学中，这种想法被保留——灵魂被归还给最初的给予者，或溶解在介于存在与非存在之间的模糊状态中。然而，灵魂首先是创造性的。它可以从许多角度讨论。它的特性可以在一定程度上被给出，事实上，如果我的读者有足够高的动机，并且这是他们的主要关注点，他们中的大多数都能自己发现这些特性。灵魂或存有本身是任何宇宙中已知的最具动机、最高能量、最强大的意识单元。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It utilizes numberless methods of perception, and it has at its command many other kinds of consciousness. Your idea of the soul is indeed limited by your three-dimensional concepts. The soul can change the focus of its consciousness, and uses consciousness as you use the eyes in your head. Now in my level of existence I am simply aware of the fact, strange as it may seem, that I am not my consciousness. My consciousness is an attribute to be used by me. This applies to each of the readers of this book, even though the knowledge may be hidden. Soul or entity, then, is more than consciousness.
quotes.translation: 它利用无数种感知方法，并且它支配着许多其他种类的意识。你们对灵魂的看法确实被你们的三维概念所限制。灵魂可以改变其意识的焦点，并像你们使用头上的眼睛一样使用意识。现在，在我存在的层面，我只是意识到一个事实，尽管这看起来可能很奇怪，即我不是我的意识。我的意识是我可以使用的一个属性。这适用于本书的每一位读者，即使这些知识可能被隐藏。因此，灵魂或存有，超越了意识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I tell you this, and at the same time remind you that your present personality is never lost. Now another word for the soul is entity. You see it is not a simple matter of giving you a definition of a soul or entity, for even to have a glimpse in logical terms you would have to understand it in spiritual, psychic, and electromagnetic terms, and understand the basic nature of consciousness and action as well. But you can intuitively discover the nature of the soul or entity, and in many ways intuitive knowledge is superior to any other kind.
quotes.translation: 我告诉你们这些，同时提醒你们，你们当前的人格永远不会丢失。现在，灵魂的另一个词是存有。你们看，这不是简单给你们一个灵魂或存有的定义，因为即使要以逻辑术语瞥见它，你们也必须从灵性、心灵和电磁的术语来理解它，并且还要理解意识和行动的基本本质。但你们可以直觉地发现灵魂或存有的本质，而且在许多方面，直觉知识优于任何其他种类的知识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It goes without saying then that the soul does not require a physical body for purposes of perception; that perception is not dependent upon physical senses; that experience continues whether or not you are in this life or another; and also that the soul's basic methods of perception are also operating within you now even as you read this book. It also follows that your experience within the physical system is dependent upon a physical form and physical senses -- again, because these interpret reality and translate it into physical data. It also follows that some hints of the soul's direct experience can be gained by momentarily switching the physical senses off -- by refusing to use them as perceptors, and falling back upon other methods. Now you do this to some extent in the dream state, but even then in many dreams you still tend to translate experience into hallucinatory physical terms. Most of the dreams that you recall are of this nature.
quotes.translation: 不言而喻，灵魂并不需要肉体来进行感知；感知并不依赖于物质感官；无论你处于此生还是另一世，体验都持续存在；而且，即使在你阅读本书的此刻，灵魂的基本感知方式也在你之内运作。同样，你在物质系统内的体验依赖于物质形态和物质感官——再次强调，因为这些将现实诠释并转化为物质数据。这也意味着，通过暂时关闭物质感官——拒绝将其作为感知工具，转而依赖其他方法——你可以获得灵魂直接体验的一些线索。你在梦境状态中在一定程度上做到了这一点，但即使在梦中，你仍倾向于将体验转化为幻觉性的物质术语。你所回忆的大多数梦境都具有这种性质。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is energy concentrated to a degree quite unbelievable to you. It contains potentials unlimited, but it must work out its own identity and form its own worlds. It carries within it the burden of all being. Within it are personality potentials beyond your comprehension. Remember, this is your own soul or entity I am speaking of, as well as soul or entity in general. You are one manifestation of your own soul. How many of you would want to limit your reality, your entire reality, to the experience you now know? You do this when you imagine that your present self is your entire personality, or insist that your identity be maintained unchanged through an endless eternity.
quotes.translation: 它是能量，其集中程度令你难以置信。它包含无限的潜能，但必须发展出自身的身份并形成自己的世界。它承载着所有存在的重担。其中蕴含着你无法理解的人格潜能。记住，我所说的是你自己的灵魂或存有，也是一般意义上的灵魂或存有。你是自己灵魂的一种显现。你们中有多少人愿意将自己的实相——你全部的实相——局限于你现在所知的体验？当你想象你现在的自我就是你的全部人格，或者坚持你的身份必须在一个无尽的永恒中保持不变时，你就在这样做。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Dogmas or systems of thought that tell you to rise above your emotions can be misleading -- even, in your terms, somewhat dangerous. Such theories are based upon the concept that there is something innately disruptive, base, or wrong in man's emotional nature, while the soul is always depicted as being calm, "perfect," passive and unfeeling. Only the most lofty, blissful awareness is allowed. Yet the soul is above all a fountain of energy, creativity, and action that shows its characteristics in life precisely through the ever-changing emotions.
quotes.translation: 那些告诉你超越情绪的信条或思想体系可能会产生误导——甚至，用你们的话说，有些危险。这类理论基于这样一种概念：人类的情感本质中天生具有破坏性、低贱或错误的东西，而灵魂则总是被描绘成平静、“完美”、被动且无感。只有最崇高、最幸福的觉知才被允许。然而，灵魂首先是能量、创造力和行动的源泉，它恰恰通过不断变化的情绪在生命中展现其特性。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In the body's spontaneous functioning you see the easy mobility of the soul, the "going with that which I am," which is an indication of the soul's inner freedom and yet innate sense of direction. All portions of the body's reality are versions in flesh of the soul's reality, even as all segments of the exterior universe mirror an internal one. The latter is as alive and natural and changing as the exterior world. Physical phenomena is only a portion of what nature is, and all realities are natural.
quotes.translation: 在身体的自发运作中，你看到了灵魂的轻松流动性，即“随我所是而行”，这体现了灵魂内在的自由和与生俱来的方向感。身体实相的所有部分都是灵魂实相在肉体中的版本，正如外部宇宙的所有片段都映照着内在宇宙。后者与外部世界一样充满活力、自然且不断变化。物质现象只是自然的一部分，而所有实相都是自然的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You think that the soul is a white wall with nothing written upon it, and so your idea of sacrilege is to shit upon it, not realizing that the shit and the soul are one, and that the biological is spiritual; and that, again -- if you will forgive my homey concept -- flowers grow from the shit of the earth. And in a true communion, all things of this life return to the earth, and are consumed and rise up again in a new life that is never destroyed or annihilated, though always changing form.
quotes.translation: 你认为灵魂是一堵白墙，上面空无一物，所以你对亵渎的看法就是在上面拉屎，却没有意识到屎与灵魂是一体的，生物性就是灵性；而且，再次——如果你能原谅我这种家常的概念——花朵从大地的屎中生长出来。在真正的交融中，此生的万物都回归大地，被消耗，并在新的生命中重新升起，这新生命永不毁灭或消亡，尽管形态不断变化。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In larger terms, my soul includes my reincarnational personalities, Seth Two, and probable selves. I am as aware of my probable selves, incidentally, as I am of my reincarnational existences. Your concept of the soul is simply so limited. I am not really speaking in terms of group souls, though this interpretation can also be made.
quotes.translation: 从更大的角度来说，我的灵魂包括我的转世人格、赛斯第二以及可能的自我。顺便说一句，我对可能的自我的了解，就像我对转世存在的了解一样。你对灵魂的概念实在太有限了。我并非真正在谈论群体灵魂，尽管这种解释也可以成立。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your own personality as you know it, that portion of you that you consider most precious, most uniquely you, will also never be destroyed or lost. It is a portion of the soul. It will not be gobbled by the soul, nor erased by it, nor subjugated by it; nor on the other hand can it ever be separated. It is, nevertheless, only one aspect of your soul. Your individuality, in whatever way you want to think of it, continues to exist in your terms.
quotes.translation: 你所认知的自身人格，你认为最珍贵、最独特的那部分自我，也永远不会被毁灭或遗失。它是灵魂的一部分。它不会被灵魂吞噬、抹除或压制；另一方面，它也永远无法与灵魂分离。然而，它只是你灵魂的一个面向。你的个体性，无论你以何种方式理解它，都会在你所认知的层面上继续存在。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You can find out what the soul is now, therefore. It is not something waiting for you at your death, nor is it something you must save or redeem, and it is also something that you cannot lose. The term, "to lose or save your soul", has been grossly misinterpreted and distorted, for it is the part of you that is indeed indestructible. We will go into this particular matter in a portion of the book dealing with religion and the god concept.
quotes.translation: 因此，你现在就能发现灵魂是什么。它并非在你死后等待你的东西，也不是你必须拯救或救赎的东西，而且它也是你无法失去的东西。“失去或拯救你的灵魂”这个说法被严重误解和扭曲了，因为它确实是你不可毁灭的那部分。我们将在本书讨论宗教与神祇概念的章节中深入探讨这个特定问题。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The soul creates the flesh. The creator hardly looks down upon its creation. The soul creates the flesh for a reason, and physical existence for a reason, so none of this is to lead you to a distaste for physical life, nor toward a lack of appreciation for those sensual joys with which you are surrounded. Any inner journeys should allow you to find greater significance, beauty, and meaning in life as you know it now; but full enjoyment and development also means that you use all of your abilities, that you explore inner dimensions with as much wonder and enthusiasm. With proper understanding, therefore, it is quite possible for you to become quite familiar now with after-death landscapes and environments and experiences. You will find them to be as vivid as any you know. Such explorations will completely alter somber preconceptions about existence after death. It is very important that you divest yourself of as many preconceptions as possible, however, for these will impede your progress.
quotes.translation: 灵魂创造了血肉。创造者绝不会轻视自己的创造。灵魂创造血肉有其理由，创造物质实相也有其理由，所以这一切都不会引导你厌恶物质生活，也不会让你缺乏对周遭感官喜悦的欣赏。任何内在旅程都应让你在当下所认知的生命中找到更重大的意义、美与价值；但充分的享受与发展也意味着你运用所有能力，以同样多的惊奇与热忱探索内在维度。因此，在正确理解下，你完全有可能现在就相当熟悉死后的风景、环境与体验。你会发现它们与你所知的任何事物一样生动。这样的探索将彻底改变对死后存在的阴郁预想。然而，让自己尽可能摆脱先入为主的观念非常重要，因为这些观念会阻碍你的进步。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your body is the basic product of your creativity on a physical level. From its integrity all other constructions in your lifetime must come. Your greatest artistic endeavors must arise out of the soul-in-flesh (with hyphens). You create yourselves on a daily basis, changing your form according to the incalculable richness of your multitudinous abilities. (Very positively:) So out of the soul's resplendent psychic richness do you spring with your free will and desire. You in turn create other living creatures. You also produce forms of art -- fluid living constructs that you do not understand, in terms of societies and civilizations -- and all of these flow through your alliance with flesh and blood.
quotes.translation: 你的身体是你在物质层面上创造力的基本产物。从它的完整性出发，你生命中所有其他建构才得以产生。你最伟大的艺术努力必须源自“肉身中的灵魂”（带连字符）。你们每天都在创造自己，根据你们众多能力难以估量的丰富性改变自己的形态。（非常肯定地：）因此，从灵魂辉煌的心灵丰饶中，你们带着自由意志与渴望涌现而出。你们又转而创造其他生物。你们也产生艺术形式——你们以社会与文明之名却不理解的流动活建构——而所有这些都通过你们与血肉的联盟流动。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: This basic unit is endowed with unpredictability. That very unpredictability allows for infinite patterns and fulfillments. The word "soul" unfortunately has been so used in regard to your species that it becomes highly difficult to unravel the conceptual difficulties. Using usual definitions, you would call a soul the result of a certain organization of such units, which you would then recognize as a "soul."
quotes.translation: 这个基本单位被赋予了不可预测性。正是这种不可预测性允许了无限的模式与实现。“灵魂”这个词不幸地被如此用于你们的物种，以至于极难解开概念上的困境。使用通常的定义，你会将灵魂称为这种单位特定组织的结果，然后你会将其认知为一个“灵魂”。

quotes.text: Souls are also creative psychic structures, ever-changing and yet always retaining individual integrity (pause), and all are dependent one upon the other. Souls make up the life of the entity in those terms. Yet the entity is "more" than the soul is. Take a break.
quotes.translation: 灵魂也是创造性的心灵结构，不断变化却始终保留个体完整性（停顿），并且所有灵魂都相互依存。就此而言，灵魂构成了存有的生命。然而，存有“大于”灵魂。休息一下。

quotes.text: He will emerge. The drugs allowed him to permit himself a luxury that he could ill afford. There are those who are so tightly meshed within physical reality that the soul is squeezed dry. They are tight, sore, and chafing beneath too severe habits and ideas. For them momentary release, such as the drugs can give, is highly beneficial. For our friend however the inner self has been overly involved with wandering, and but lightly held within the limits of the intense focus demanded by physical reality. This freedom catered to his weakness, you see. Had he more discipline it could also have released his strength.
quotes.translation: 他会浮现出来。药物让他得以纵容自己一种他本无力负担的奢侈。有些人如此紧密地嵌在物质实相中，以至于灵魂被榨干。他们紧绷、疼痛，在过于严苛的习惯和观念下摩擦不适。对他们而言，药物所能给予的短暂释放极为有益。然而对我们的朋友来说，内在自我过度涉入漫游，只是被轻握在物质实相所要求的强烈聚焦的界限内。你看，这种自由助长了他的弱点。若他更有纪律，它也能释放他的力量。

quotes.text: It was an attempt to fit man within the picture of evolution, and to manufacture a creature whose very existence was somehow pitted against itself. Evolutionary man, with Darwinian roots, could not be a creature with a soul. It had to have hidden in its psychological roots the bloody remnants of the struggle for survival that now cast it in its uneasy role. (Pause.) There is no doubt that the church cast the soul in a position of stress, caught as it was between its heavenly source and original sin -- but there was a sense of psychological mobility involved, one that saw continued existence after death.
quotes.translation: 这是试图将人纳入进化图景中，并制造出一种其存在本身便与自身对立的生物。带有达尔文根源的进化人，不可能是拥有灵魂的生物。其心理根源中必然隐藏着生存斗争的血腥残余，这残余如今将其置于不安的角色中。（停顿。）无疑，教会将灵魂置于压力之下，使其夹在天堂源头与原罪之间——但这其中涉及一种心理上的流动性，一种看到死后持续存在的流动性。

quotes.text: As I have certainly hinted, the body is a miraculous organism, and you have barely learned the most simple of its structures.1 You do not understand the properties of soul or body, yet the body was given to you so that you could learn from it. The properties of the earth are meant to lead you into the nature of the soul. You create physical reality, yet without knowing how you do so, so that the wondrous structure of the earth itself is meant to lead you to question your own source. Nature as you understand it is meant to be your teacher. You are not its master.
quotes.translation: 正如我肯定暗示过的，身体是一个神奇的有机体，而你们几乎连其最简单的结构都尚未学会。你们不了解灵魂或身体的特性，然而身体被赐予你们，是为了让你们能从中学习。大地的特性旨在引导你们进入灵魂的本质。你们创造物质实相，却不知如何做到，因此大地本身奇妙的构造旨在引导你们质疑自己的源头。你们所理解的自然，意在成为你们的老师。你们不是它的主人。

quotes.text: In all cases when you are concerned about your health, there is a choice of directions for you to follow. The living flesh is yours. It is the materialization of your soul, and through the body the soul will provide you with those answers you require. In the next chapter we will begin to discuss those methods that can be used to refresh and heal the body, and that will help you arouse from within the physical form those memories and experiences most to your advantage. For best results, you must remember that ideas are as alive as the cells within your hand.
quotes.translation: 在所有情况下，当你们关心自己的健康时，都有方向可供选择。活生生的血肉是你们的。它是你们灵魂的物质化，通过身体，灵魂将提供你们所需的答案。在下一章中，我们将开始讨论那些可用于更新和疗愈身体的方法，这些方法将帮助你们从物质形态内部唤起那些最有益的记忆与经验。为获最佳效果，你们必须记住，观念与你们手中的细胞一样鲜活。

quotes.text: Each "part" of the soul contains the whole -- a concept I am sure will startle you. As you become more aware of your own subjective reality you will therefore, become familiar with greater portions of your own soul. When you think of the soul as a closed system you perceive it as such, and close off from yourself the knowledge of its greater creativity and characteristics.
quotes.translation: 灵魂的每个“部分”都包含整体——我相信这个概念会让你们震惊。随着你们对自己的主观实相更加觉察，你们将因此熟悉自己灵魂的更大部分。当你们将灵魂视为一个封闭系统时，你们便如此感知它，并切断了对自己灵魂更大创造力与特性的认知。

quotes.text: In physical life the soul is clothed in chemicals, and you will use the ingredients you take into your body to form an image that is in line with your beliefs. Some of these ideas will undoubtedly be accepted by you from your culture. Others will be your own private interpretation of yourself in flesh. Your beliefs about any chemical will affect what it does to you. Under LSD therapy you expect a drastic reaction and are told to prepare yourself. Your experience will follow your beliefs and your therapist's, communicated verbally and telepathically.
quotes.translation: 在物质生命中，灵魂披上化学物质的外衣，你们将利用摄入体内的成分，形成与你们信念一致的影像。其中一些观念无疑会从你们的文化中被接受。另一些则是你们对自己在肉身中的私人诠释。你们对任何化学物质的信念，都会影响它对你的作用。在LSD疗法下，你们期待剧烈反应，并被要求做好准备。你们的体验将遵循你们的信念以及治疗师通过语言和心电感应传达的信念。

quotes.text: When you are proficient you will not be swept willy-nilly into other stages of consciousness as you sleep, but will be able to understand and direct these activities. Consciousness is an attribute of the soul, a tool that can be turned in many directions. You are not your consciousness. It is something that belongs to you and to the soul. You are learning to use it. To the extent that you understand and utilize the various aspects of consciousness, you will learn to understand your own reality, and the conscious self will truly become conscious.
quotes.translation: 当你熟练时，你将不会在睡眠中被动地卷入其他意识阶段，而是能够理解并引导这些活动。意识是灵魂的一种属性，是可以转向许多方向的工具。你不是你的意识。它是属于你和灵魂的东西。你正在学习使用它。当你理解和运用意识的各个层面时，你将学会理解自己的实相，而意识自我将真正变得有意识。

quotes.text: (Intently:) You often find yourself encountering your own structures, no longer hidden in the kind of experience with which you are familiar. These may then appear in quite a different light. You may be convinced that you are evil simply because you are physical. You may believe that the soul "descends" into the body, and therefore that the body is lower, inferior, and a degraded version of "what you really are." At the same time your own physical being knows better, and basically cannot accept such a concept.2 So in daily life you may project this idea of unworth outward onto another person, who seems then to be your enemy; or upon another nation. In general, you might select animals to play the part of the enemy, or members of another religion, or political parties.
quotes.translation: （专注地：）你常常发现自己遇到自己的结构，它们不再隐藏在你所熟悉的那种经验中。这些结构可能以完全不同的面貌出现。你可能仅仅因为自己是物质性的就确信自己是邪恶的。你可能相信灵魂"降入"身体，因此身体是低等、卑劣的，是"你真正所是"的退化版本。同时，你的物质存在本身更明白真相，基本上无法接受这样的概念。所以在日常生活中，你可能将这种无价值感投射到另一个人身上，那人似乎就成了你的敌人；或者投射到另一个国家。一般来说，你可能会选择动物来扮演敌人的角色，或者选择其他宗教或政党的成员。

quotes.text: The trouble is that you frequently consider the soul or entity as a finished, static "thing" that belongs to you but is not you. The soul or entity -- in other words, your most intimate powerful inner identity -- is and must be forever changing. It is not, therefore, something like a cherished heirloom. It is alive, responsive, curious. It forms the flesh and the world that you know, and it is in a state of becoming.
quotes.translation: 问题在于，你常常将灵魂或存有视为一个完成、静态的"东西"，它属于你但又不是你。灵魂或存有——换言之，你最亲密、最强大的内在身份——必须且永远在变化中。因此，它不像一件珍贵的传家宝。它是活的、有回应的、好奇的。它形成了你所知的肉身和世界，并且处于一种"变成"的状态中。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: There are far more wonders to perceive through this inward exploration than you can possibly believe until you begin such a journey for yourself. You are a soul; you are a particular manifestation of a soul, and it is sheer nonsense to think that you must remain ignorant of the nature of your own being. You may not be able to put your knowledge clearly into words, but this will in no way negate the value or the validity of the experience that will be yours once you begin to look inward.
quotes.translation: 通过这种向内探索，你能感知到的奇迹远比你在开始这样的旅程之前所能相信的要多得多。你是一个灵魂；你是灵魂的一个特定显现，认为你必须对自己存在的本质保持无知，这完全是胡说八道。你可能无法清晰地将你的知识用语言表达出来，但这绝不会否定一旦你开始向内看时，你所获得的经验的价值或有效性。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: You run into many contradictions. God is supposed to be male. The soul is sometimes considered female. The angels are male. Now let us look at the Garden of Eden. The story says that Eve tempted the male, having him eat of the tree of good and evil, or the tree of knowledge. (Pause.) This represented a state of consciousness, the point at which the species began to think and feel for itself, when it approached a certain state of consciousness in which it dared exert its own creativity.
quotes.translation: 你会遇到许多矛盾。上帝被认为是男性。灵魂有时被认为是女性。天使是男性。现在让我们看看伊甸园。故事说夏娃引诱了男性，让他吃了善恶知识树的果实。（停顿。）这代表了一种意识状态，即物种开始为自己思考和感受的时刻，当它接近某种敢于发挥自身创造力的意识状态时。

quotes.text: For the thought shall seed the world and the world shall seed the universe. The cowering soul is, indeed, the soul who believes himself a physical being. He is therefore at the mercy of every physical camouflage. He does not realize that he is what he is. He must live in the world he has created, and it is a miserable and cruel world.
quotes.translation: 因为思想将播种世界，而世界将播种宇宙。畏缩的灵魂，确实是那个相信自己是物质存在的灵魂。因此，他受制于每一个物质幻相。他没有意识到他就是他所是。他必须活在他所创造的世界里，而那是一个悲惨而残酷的世界。

quotes.text: The whole self contains the ego, and he ignored this. Any investigations into the nature of existence, and any true learning, must take place within all levels of the self. He cut off his ego to save his soul, and nearly lost both in the bargain. The ego indeed is part of the soul. It is one of the voices of the soul. (Long pause.)
quotes.translation: 整体自体包含自我，但他忽略了这一点。任何对存在本质的探究，以及任何真正的学习，都必须在自体的所有层面上进行。他切断自我以拯救灵魂，结果几乎两者皆失。自我确实是灵魂的一部分。它是灵魂的声音之一。（长停顿。）

quotes.text: You, dear reader, are in the process of expanding your psychic structure, [of] becoming a conscious participator with the soul, in certain terms, [of] becoming what your soul is. As cells multiply and grow -- within their own nature and the physical framework -- so do selves "evolve" in terms of value fulfillment.*
quotes.translation: 亲爱的读者，你正在扩展你的心灵结构，在某种程度上成为灵魂的有意识参与者，成为你的灵魂所是的样子。正如细胞在其本性和物质框架内繁殖和成长，自体也在价值完成*的意义上“进化”。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: The soul or entity is always in a state of flux, or learning, and of developments that have to do with subjective experience rather than with time or space. This is not nearly as mysterious as it might sound. Each of my readers plays a game in which the egotistical conscious self pretends not to know what the whole self definitely does know. Since the ego is definitely a part of the whole self, then it must necessarily be basically aware of such knowledge. In its intense focus in physical reality, however, it pretends not to know, until it feels able to utilize the information in physical terms.
quotes.translation: 灵魂或存有始终处于流动、学习以及与主观体验相关的发展状态，而非与时间或空间相关。这并不像听起来那么神秘。我的每位读者都在玩一个游戏，其中自我意识假装不知道整体自体确实知道的事情。既然自我无疑是整体自体的一部分，那么它必然在根本上知晓这些知识。然而，由于它强烈聚焦于物质实相，它假装不知道，直到它感觉能够以物质形式利用这些信息。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This tale, I admit, is far more difficult to understand than a simple story of God's creation of the world, or its actual production in a meaningless universe through the slippery hands of chance--and yet my story is more magnificent because elements of its truth will find resonance in the minds and hearts of those open enough to listen. For men's minds themselves are alive with the desire to read properly, and they are aware of their own vast heritage. It is not simply that man has a soul that is somehow blessed while the rest of him is not, but that in those terms everything [he knows], regardless of size or degree, is made of "soul stuff." Each portion has its own identity and validity--and no portion is ever annihilated or destroyed. The form may change.
quotes.translation: 我承认，这个故事比一个简单的上帝创造世界的故事，或世界在无意义宇宙中通过偶然之手实际产生的故事，要难理解得多——然而我的故事更为壮丽，因为其真相的元素会在那些足够开放去聆听的人的心灵中找到共鸣。因为人类的心灵本身就充满了正确解读的渴望，并且他们意识到自己庞大的遗产。问题不仅仅在于人类拥有一个以某种方式被祝福的灵魂而其余部分则不然，而是在那些术语中，他所知道的一切，无论大小或程度，都是由“灵魂材料”构成的。每个部分都有其自身的身份和有效性——没有任何部分会被消灭或摧毁。形式可能会改变。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Consciousness is not basically built upon those precepts of good and evil that so presently concern you. By inference, neither is a soul. This does not mean that in your system, and in some others, these problems do not exist and that good is not preferable to the evil. It simply means that the soul knows that good and evil are but different manifestations of a far greater reality.
quotes.translation: 意识并非基本上建立在那些当前如此困扰你们的善恶戒律之上。由此推论，灵魂也不是。这并不意味着在你们的系统以及其他一些系统中，这些问题不存在，也不意味着善不比恶更可取。这仅仅意味着灵魂知道善与恶不过是一个更伟大实相的不同表现。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It exists then, "eternally," separated from the physical clothing that you need in order to understand it. Physical existence is one way in which the soul chooses to experience its own actuality. The soul, in other words, has created a world for you to inhabit, to change -- a complete sphere of activity in which new developments and indeed new forms of consciousness can emerge.
quotes.translation: 因此，它“永恒地”存在，与你们为了理解它而需要的物质外衣相分离。物质存在是灵魂选择体验自身实相的一种方式。换句话说，灵魂为你们创造了一个世界来居住、改变——一个完整的活动领域，在其中新的发展乃至新的意识形式可以出现。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There is no need to create a separate god who exists outside of your universe and separate from it, nor is there any need to think of a soul as some distant entity. God, or All That Is, is intimately a part of you. "His" energy forms your identity, and your soul is a part of you in the same manner.
quotes.translation: 没有必要创造一个存在于你的宇宙之外并与它分离的独立神祇，也没有必要将灵魂视为某种遥远的实体。神，或一切万有，亲密地是你的一部分。"祂"的能量构成了你的身份，而你的灵魂以同样的方式是你的一部分。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: First of all, a soul is not something that you have. It is what you are. I usually use the term "entity" in preference to the term "soul," simply because those particular misconceptions are not so connected with the word "entity," and its connotations are less religious in an organizational sense.
quotes.translation: 首先，灵魂不是你拥有的东西。它就是你所是的。我通常更喜欢用"存有"一词而非"灵魂"，仅仅是因为那些特定的误解与"存有"这个词关联较少，且其含义在组织性宗教层面上的色彩较淡。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The practical nature of his own dreams was also more apparent, for again, his dreams sent him precise visions as to where food might be located, for example, and for some centuries there were human migrations of a kind that now you see the geese make. All of those journeys followed literal paths that were given as information in the dream state. [But] more and more man began to identify himself with his exterior environment. He began to think of his inner ego almost as if it were a stranger to himself. It became his version of the soul, and there seemed to be a duality--a self who acted in the physical universe, and a separate spiritlike soul that acted in an immaterial world.
quotes.translation: 他自身梦境的实用本质也更为明显，因为举例来说，他的梦境会给他精确的幻象，指示食物可能位于何处。在几个世纪里，曾有过人类迁徙，其方式正如你现在看到的大雁迁徙。所有这些旅程都遵循着在梦境状态中作为信息给出的实际路径。[但]人类越来越开始将自己与外在环境等同起来。他开始把内在自我几乎视为一个陌生人。这成了他版本的灵魂，于是似乎出现了一种二元性——一个在物质宇宙中行动的自我，和一个在非物质世界中行动的、类似精神的独立灵魂。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Other concepts are really not basically workable even in your own physical reality. A rigid, dogmatic concept of good and evil will force you to perceive physical existence as a battleground of opposing forces, with the poor unwary soul almost as a buffer. Or you will think of the poor soul as a blackboard eraser, slapped between two hands -- one good and one evil.
quotes.translation: 其他概念实际上即使在你们自己的物质实相中也基本行不通。一个僵化、教条的善恶观念会迫使你将物质存在视为对立力量的战场，而可怜、毫无防备的灵魂几乎成了缓冲器。或者你会把可怜的灵魂想成一块黑板擦，被拍打在两只手掌之间——一只是善，一只是恶。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 719

This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Now personality, even as you know it, constantly changes, and not always in ways that are anticipated -- most often, in fact, in unpredictable ways. You insist upon focusing your attention upon the similarities that are woven through your own behavior; and upon these you build a theory that the self follows a pattern that you, instead, have transposed upon it. And the transposed pattern prevents you from seeing the self as it really is. Therefore, you also project this distorted viewpoint upon your conception of the reality of the soul. You think of the soul, therefore, in the light of erroneous conceptions that you hold regarding even the nature of your mortal selves.
quotes.translation: 人格，即使如你所知，也在不断变化，而且并非总是以可预期的方式——事实上，最常见的是以不可预测的方式。你坚持将注意力集中在贯穿你自身行为的相似性上；并基于这些相似性构建了一个理论，认为自我遵循着某种模式，而实际上是你将这个模式转嫁到了自我之上。这个被转嫁的模式阻止你看到自我的真实面目。因此，你也将这个扭曲的观点投射到你对灵魂实相的概念上。因此，你根据甚至对你凡俗自我本质所持有的错误观念来思考灵魂。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Some individuals find themselves with memories of other lives, which are other days to the soul. Such persons then become aware of a greater consciousness reaching over those gaps, and realize that earthly experience can contain [among other things] a knowledge of existence in more than one body. Inherently then consciousness, affiliated with the flesh, can indeed carry such comprehensions. The mind of man as you know it shows at least the potential ability for handling a kind of memory with which you are usually not acquainted. This means that even biologically the species is equipped to deal with different sequences of time, while still manipulating within one particular time scheme. This also implies a far greater psychological richness -- quite possible, again, within corporal reality -- in which many levels of relationships can be handled. Such inner knowledge is inherent in the cells, and in ordinary terms of evolution is quite possible as a "future" development.
quotes.translation: 有些人发现自己拥有其他生命的记忆，这些生命对灵魂而言如同其他日子。这样的人于是会意识到一种跨越那些间隙的更大意识，并认识到世俗经验可以包含[除其他外]对存在于不止一个身体中的认知。因此，与血肉相连的意识，其内在确实能够承载这样的理解。如你所知的人类心智，至少显示出处理一种你通常不熟悉的记忆的潜在能力。这意味着，即使在生物学上，这个物种也装备了处理不同时间序列的能力，同时仍能在某个特定时间框架内运作。这也暗示了一种远为丰富的心理层面——再次，这在肉体实相内是完全可能的——在其中可以处理许多层次的关系。这种内在知识是细胞固有的，并且用通常的进化术语来说，作为一项"未来的"发展是完全可能的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your ideas of sexuality follow both your religions and your sciences, then, for you have created each. But you always know what you are doing, and there are cycles in the earth, and in your being and your soul. And so you are in the process of reuniting yourself, and discovering what the word "humanity" means. You are finding out the meaning of individuality, which is a far more important word than you realize, and when you understand that meaning, then your own individuality will express itself in its natural form. Regardless of what words are put upon your experience in terms of sexual roles, you will be a full human being. When the conscious and the unconscious minds are understood, you will have no more problems with sexuality.
quotes.translation: 你们对性的观念既遵循宗教也遵循科学，因为这两者都是你们创造的。但你们始终清楚自己在做什么，地球、你们的存有与灵魂都有其周期。因此你们正处在重新整合自我的过程中，并逐渐领悟"人性"一词的真义。你们正在发现个体性的真谛——这个词远比你们意识到的更为重要——当你们理解其含义时，你们的个体性将以自然形态展现。无论外界用何种词汇定义你们的性别角色经验，你们都将成为完整的人。当有意识与无意识心智被理解时，你们将不再受困于性的问题。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I am not saying that words cannot be used to describe the psyche, but they cannot define it. It is futile to question: "What is the difference between my psyche and my soul, my entity and my greater being?" for all of these are terms used in an effort to express the greater portions of your own experience that you sense within yourself. Your use of language may make you impatient for definitions, however. Hopefully this book will allow you some intimate awareness, some definite experience, that will acquaint you with the nature of your own psyche, and then you will see that its reality escapes all definitions, defies all categorizing, and shoves aside with exuberant creativity all attempts to wrap it up in a neat package.
quotes.translation: 我并非说语言无法描述心灵，但它无法定义心灵。追问"我的心灵与灵魂、我的存有与更高自我有何区别"是徒劳的——所有这些术语都是用来表达你们内在感知到的更宏大经验。然而语言的使用可能使你们急于寻求定义。希望这本书能让你们获得某些亲密觉知、某些确凿体验，使你们熟悉自身心灵的本质，届时你们将明白：它的实相超越所有定义，抗拒一切分类，并以蓬勃的创造力将所有试图将其装进整齐包装的企图抛诸脑后。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now I do not mean this to be merely a symbolic exercise, for though it may begin with imagination, it is based upon fact, and emanations from your consciousness and the creativity of your soul do indeed reach outward in that manner. The exercise will give you some idea of the true nature, creativity, and vitality of the soul from which you can draw your own energy and of which you are an individual and unique portion.
quotes.translation: 我并非说这仅是象征性练习——尽管它始于想象，但基于事实。你们意识散发出的能量与灵魂的创造力确实以这种方式向外延伸。这个练习将使你们了解灵魂的真实本质、创造力与生命力，你们从中汲取自身能量，而你们正是其中独特而不可分割的部分。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your present methods will simply bring you pat, manufactured results and answers. They will satisfy neither the intellect nor the soul. Since your universe springs from an inner one, and since that inner one pervades each nook and cranny of your own existence, you must look where you have not before -- into the reality of your own minds and emotions. You must look to the natural universe that you know. You must look with your intuitions and creative instincts at the creatures about you, seeing them not as other species with certain habits, not as inferior properties of the earth, to be dissected, but as living examples of the nature of the universe, in constant being and transformation.
quotes.translation: 你们当前的方法只会带来刻板的人造结果与答案，既无法满足智性，也无法满足灵魂。既然你们的宇宙源于内在宇宙，而内在宇宙渗透于你们存在的每个角落，你们必须探索从未涉足之处——进入自己心智与情绪的真实。你们必须观察所知的自然宇宙，以直觉与创造本能注视周遭生物，不再视其为具有特定习性的异类物种，或可被解剖的地球低级财产，而是宇宙本质的鲜活例证，处于永恒的存有与转化之中。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The same applies to each reader of this book. The soul is open-ended, therefore. It is not a closed spiritual or psychic system. I have tried to show you that the soul is not a separate, apart-from-you thing. It is no more divorced from you than -- capital -- God is.
quotes.translation: 本书每位读者亦复如是。因此灵魂是开放的，并非封闭的灵性或心灵系统。我试图向你们证明：灵魂并非与你们分离的独立存在。它与你们的距离，并不比——大写——神与你们的距离更远。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Entities or souls, in other words, send out portions of themselves to open up avenues of reality that would not exist otherwise. (Long pause at 9:39.) The three-dimensional selves, in existing within these realities, must focus their attention there completely. An inner awareness gives them a source of energy and strength. They must, however, come to understand their roles as actors, "finally" from their roles, and through another act of comprehension, return to the entity.
quotes.translation: 换言之，存有或灵魂会派出自身部分，开辟原本不存在的实相通道。（9:39分长暂停）三维自我存在于这些实相中时，必须将注意力完全聚焦于此。内在觉知赋予它们能量与力量的源泉。但它们必须理解自身作为演员的角色，"最终"从角色中抽离，并通过另一重领悟，回归存有。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This book is Seth's way of demonstrating that human personality is multidimensional, that we exist in many realities at once, that the soul or inner self is not something apart from us, but the very medium in which we exist. He emphasizes that "truth" is not found by going from teacher to teacher, church to church, or discipline to discipline, but by looking within the self. The intimate knowledge of consciousness, the "secrets of the universe," are not esoteric truths to be hidden from the people, then. Such information is as natural to man as air, and as available to those who honestly seek it by looking to the source within.
quotes.translation: 这本书是赛斯展示人类人格具有多维性、我们同时存在于多重实相、灵魂或内在自我并非与我们分离之物，而是我们赖以存在的媒介本身的方式。他强调"真理"并非通过辗转于导师、教会或法门之间获得，而是通过向内观照自我。对意识的深层认知、"宇宙的奥秘"并非秘而不宣的深奥真理。这类信息对人类而言如同空气般自然，凡诚心向内求索者皆可触及。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth began dictating the book in our next Session 511, January 21, 1970, and finished it in Session 591, August 11, 1971. The intervening sessions did not all involve book dictation, however. Some were devoted to personal matters, some given for specific people who needed help, and some were in answer to philosophical questions not connected with the book. I also took several "little vacations." Despite such layoffs, Seth always picked right up, precisely where he left off dictation.

quotes.text: At certain depths of sleep, however, the soul's perception operates relatively unhampered. You drink, so to speak, from the pure well of perception. You communicate with the depths of your own being, and the source of your creativity. These experiences, not being translated physically, do not remain in the morning. You do not remember them as dreams. Dreams, however, may later the same evening be formed from the information gained during what I will call the "depth experience." These will not be exact or near translations of the experience, but rather of the nature of dream parables -- an entirely different thing, you see.
quotes.translation: 然而在特定深度的睡眠中，灵魂的感知运作相对不受阻碍。可以说，你正从感知的纯净之井中汲取养分。你与自身存在的深处及创造力的源头进行交流。这些体验因未转化为物质形态，故不会留存至清晨。你不会将它们作为梦境记起。但当晚稍后，从我称之为"深度体验"中获得的信息可能形成梦境。这些梦境并非体验的精确或近似转化，而是具有梦喻性质——这完全是另一回事，你明白吗。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The "you" who is capable of such expansion must be a far more creative and multidimensional personality than you earlier imagined. Many of the suggested small exercises given earlier in the book will also help you become acquainted with your own reality, will give you direct experience with the nature of your own soul or entity, and will put you in contact with those portions of your being from which your own vitality springs. You may or may not have your own encounters with past reincarnational selves or probable selves. You may or may not catch yourselves in the act of changing levels of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 能够实现如此扩展的"你"，必定是比你先前想象的更具创造性与多维性的人格。本书前文建议的许多小型练习，也将助你认识自身实相，让你直接体验灵魂或存有的本质，并让你接触自身生命力所源出的那些存在部分。你或许会遭遇过去转世自我或可能自我，也可能不会。你或许能捕捉到自身转换意识层次的瞬间，也可能不能。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (11:44.) The features in a portrait are painted on canvas or board, but your soul is not painted on your body. It enters into and becomes part of it. Physically, you cannot contain all of your identity, and that "free" portion unconsciously creates the flesh, in your terms. Again, you direct its form through your beliefs, but the unconscious part of you does the "work" of producing it.
quotes.translation: （11:44）肖像画的特征绘于画布或木板上，但你的灵魂并非绘制于身体之上。它进入并成为身体的一部分。从物质层面而言，你无法容纳全部身份认同，而那个"自由"的部分在你们看来无意识地创造着肉身。再次强调，你通过信念引导其形态，但你的无意识部分执行着制造它的"工作"。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The various levels of consciousness discussed here may appear to be very divorced from ordinary waking ones. The divisions are quite arbitrary. These various stages all represent different attributes and directions inherent within your own soul; clues and hints of them, shadows and reflections appear even in the consciousness that you know. Even normal waking consciousness, then, is not innocent of all other traces of existence, or devoid of other kinds of awareness. It is only because you usually use your waking consciousness in limited ways that you do not encounter these clues with any regularity.
quotes.translation: 此处讨论的各类意识层次，看似与寻常清醒意识相去甚远。这些划分相当随意。这些不同阶段皆代表你灵魂内在固有的不同属性与方向；其线索与暗示、阴影与映照甚至显现在你所知的意识中。因此，即便是正常的清醒意识，也并非全然不染其他存在的痕迹，或缺乏其他类型的觉知。只因你通常以有限方式运用清醒意识，才未能规律性地遭遇这些线索。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Channels, psychological and psychic, always exist, sending communications back and forth through the various levels of the self, and the ego accepts necessary information and data from inner portions of the personality without question. Its position in fact depends in a large manner upon this unquestioning acceptance of inner data. The ego, in other words, the "exterior" self that you think of as your self -- that portion of you maintains its safety and its seeming command precisely because inner layers of your own personality constantly uphold it, keep the physical body operating, and maintain communications with the multitudinous stimuli that come both from outside conditions and inside conditions. The soul or entity is not diminished but expanded through reincarnations, through existence and experience in probable realities -- something that I will explain later.
quotes.translation: 心理与灵性的通道始终存在，在自我的各个层次间来回传递信息，而自我毫无质疑地接受来自人格内在部分的必要信息与数据。事实上，其地位在很大程度上正依赖于这种对内在数据的不加质疑的接纳。换言之，自我——你视为"自我"的那个"外在"自我——它之所以能维持安全与表面掌控，恰恰因为你人格的内在层次持续支撑着它，维持身体运作，并与来自外在环境与内在条件的无数刺激保持沟通。灵魂或存有不会因转世、在可能实相中的存在与体验而缩减，反而得以扩展——这一点我稍后会解释。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: No man or woman consciously knows for sure which day will be the last for him or her in this particular life, that each calls the present one. Mortality with its birth and death is the framework in which the soul, for now, is expressed in flesh. Birth and death contain between them the earthly experience that you perceive as happening within a given period of time, through various seasons, and involving unique perceptions within areas of space -- encountered with other human beings, all to one extent or another sharing with you events caused by the intersection of the self and time and space.
quotes.translation: 没有任何男人或女人能确切知道此生哪一天会是自己的最后一天，每个人都把当下称为"现在"。生死构成的凡俗框架，正是灵魂此刻借以在血肉中展现的载体。出生与死亡之间，承载着你所感知的尘世体验——它发生在特定时间段内，历经四季更迭，涉及空间领域中的独特感知——在与他人相遇的过程中，所有人都在某种程度上与你共享着由自我、时间与空间交汇所引发的事件。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The inner world of each man and woman is connected with the inner world of the earth. The spirit becomes flesh. Part of each individual's soul, then, is intimately connected with what we will call the world's soul, or the soul of the earth.
quotes.translation: 每个男人和女人的内在世界都与地球的内在世界相连。精神化为血肉。因此，每个个体灵魂的一部分，都与我们所谓的"世界灵魂"或"地球灵魂"紧密相连。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The inner self, which has been called the soul, has connections through the entire physical organism, and is not concentrated in any one portion. More adjustments are made by the physical organism than you know, and when I say that it exists in many fields, I mean that it actually not only appears within them, but is a part of them in an intimate manner, that the physical organism as it is materialized within your universe is actually a coming together and merging that has its existence, and is a blending of data from many planes, that would be considered foreign by the intellect.
quotes.translation: 被称为灵魂的内在自体，通过整个物质有机体产生联结，并不集中于任何特定部位。物质有机体所做的调整远超你所知，当我说它存在于多个场域时，意味着它不仅出现在这些场域中，更以亲密的方式成为它们的一部分——这个在你们宇宙中物质化的有机体，实际上是来自多个层面的数据汇聚与融合，其存在本身是这些数据的交融，而这些数据在理智看来会是陌生的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You can readily see, therefore, how important your subjective feelings really are. This knowledge -- that your universe is idea construction -- can immediately give you clues that enable you to change your environment and circumstances beneficially. When you do not understand the nature of the soul, and do not realize that your thoughts and feelings form physical reality, then you feel powerless to change it. In later chapters of this book, I hope to give you some practical information that will enable you to alter practically the very nature and structure of daily life.
quotes.translation: 因此你很容易看出，你的主观感受究竟有多重要。这个认知——即你的宇宙是意念的建构——能立即给你线索，让你得以有益地改变环境与处境。当你不了解灵魂的本质，不明白你的思想与情感塑造了物质实相时，你就会感到无力改变它。在本书后续章节中，我希望能提供一些实用信息，让你能实际改变日常生活的本质与结构。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: This characteristic of materializing thoughts and emotions into physical realities is an attribute of the soul. Now in your reality, these thoughts are made physical. In other realities, they may be "constructed" in an entirely different fashion. So your soul, that which you are, constructs your physical daily reality for you from the nature of your thoughts and expectations.
quotes.translation: 将思想与情感物质化为实相的特性，是灵魂的属性。在你们的实相中，这些思想被转化为物质形态。在其他实相中，它们可能以完全不同的方式被"建构"。因此，你的灵魂——即你的本质——根据你思想与期望的本质，为你建构出每日的物质实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The most private agonies of the soul were assigned a more or less common source in man's primitive "unconscious" drives. The private unquelled thrusts toward creativity were seen as the unbalanced conglomeration of chemicals within a person's most private being -- a twist of perversity. Genius was seen as a mistake of chromosomes, or the fortunate result of a man's hatred for his father. The meaning of life was reduced to the accidental nature of genes. Science thought in terms of averages and statistics, and each person was supposed to fit within those realms.
quotes.translation: 灵魂最私密的痛苦，被归因于人类原始"无意识"驱力中某种共同的源头。那些未被驯服的、朝向创造力的私人冲动，被视为一个人最私密存在中化学物质的失衡组合——一种扭曲的乖戾。天才被看作染色体的错误，或一个人对父亲憎恨的幸运结果。生命的意义被简化为基因的偶然性。科学以平均值与统计数字思考，每个人都应该被框限在这些范畴之内。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The soul is not frightened for its identity. It is sure of itself. It ever seeks. It is not afraid of being overwhelmed by experience or perception. If you had a more thorough understanding of the nature of identity you would not, for example, fear telepathy, for behind this concern is the worry that your identity will be swept away by the suggestions or thoughts of others.
quotes.translation: 灵魂并不畏惧其身份认同。它对自己充满确信。它永远在追寻。它不害怕被经验或感知淹没。如果你对身份的本质有更透彻的理解，就不会恐惧心灵感应，因为这种担忧背后隐藏着对自我身份会被他人建议或思想冲垮的焦虑。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Instead, the soul stands at the center of itself, exploring, extending its capacities in all directions at once, involved in issues of creativity, each one highly legitimate. The probable system of reality opens up the nature of the soul to you. It should change current religion's ideas considerably. For this reason, the nature of good and evil is a highly important point.
quotes.translation: 相反，灵魂立于自身中心，同时向所有方向探索、扩展其能力，投身于创造性的议题中，每个议题都极具正当性。可能实相系统向你揭示了灵魂的本质。它应当大幅改变当前宗教的观念。正因如此，善恶的本质是极为重要的关键点。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Christianity for many centuries served as an amazingly creative organizational framework, that expressed the vast complexity of the soul's reality. It also in its way managed to even focus some of man's less handsome attributes toward ends that were less reprehensible than in the past. Master events of that particular nature bring about a completely new interpretation of historic events. Their intensity, power, and seemingly impelling nature exist precisely because their origins are not physical, but are drawn from the psyche's deepest resources.
quotes.translation: 基督教在多个世纪里充当了惊人创造性的组织架构，表达了灵魂实相的浩瀚复杂性。它甚至以自身方式将人类某些不那么光彩的特质导向比过去更少遭人谴责的结局。这类特定性质的主事件会带来对历史事件的全新诠释。其强度、力量与看似不可抗拒的特质，恰恰源于它们并非源自物质层面，而是从心灵最深处资源中汲取。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now: Remember in your portrait the sense of play, and a light hand. Otherwise you lose your sparkling colors and end up with a murkiness that you do not intend. Remember that the soul is plain behind the facade that you see -- that even the body is in a constant state of almost magical activity, even though as you paint it in its chair, it is physically motionless.
quotes.translation: 现在：记住在你的画像中保留游戏感与轻灵笔触。否则你会失去璀璨色彩，最终陷入非本意的混沌。记住灵魂在你所见表象之下是朴素的——即使身体处于近乎魔幻的持续活动状态，即便当你描绘它静坐椅中时，它物理上纹丝不动。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The experience that you wrote of was significant on several levels, and of course was meant to reassure you ahead of time because of the events you knew would occur. The experience was to inform you emotionally and spiritually of the great meaning of each individual, portray the lovely brilliance that is within each human being, and let you know that the integrity of the self and the soul exists beyond the possibility of annihilation, as you yourself will continue to exist regardless of which path you choose to take -- dying within two years, or living physically on for many more. In other words, you will continue to exist and to be fulfilled within that love you sensed.
quotes.translation: 你所记述的经历在多个层面意义重大，当然也意在提前安抚你，因你已知晓即将发生的事件。这经历旨在从情感与灵性层面告知你每个个体的伟大意义，描绘每个人内在蕴含的可爱光辉，让你明白自我与灵魂的完整性存在于湮灭可能性之外——正如无论你选择哪条道路（两年内离世或继续肉身存活多年），你自身都将持续存在。换言之，你将在所感知的那份爱中持续存在并得到圆满。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Now it is nearly impossible to separate a discussion of the nature of the soul from a discussion of the nature of perception. Very briefly let us review a few points: You form physical matter and the physical world that you know. The physical senses actually can be said to create the physical world, in that they force you to perceive an available field of energy in physical terms, and impose a highly specialized pattern upon this field of reality. Using the physical senses, you can perceive reality in no other way.
quotes.translation: 现在几乎不可能将灵魂本质的讨论与感知本质的讨论分开。让我们简要回顾几点：你形成了物质实相和你所知的物质世界。实际上可以说物质感官创造了物质世界，因为它们迫使你以物质术语感知可用的能量场，并将高度专门化的模式强加于这个实相场。使用物质感官时，你无法以其他方式感知实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Though I yelled at my children and screamed sometimes in rage against the elements, I was struck through with the magnificence of existence, and learned more about true spirituality than I ever did as a monk. This does not mean that poverty leads to truth, or that suffering is good for the soul. Many who shared those conditions with me learned little. It does mean that each of you choose those life conditions that you have for your own purpose, knowing ahead of time where your weaknesses and strengths lie. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 尽管我曾对孩子们大吼大叫，有时在狂怒中对着天地嘶喊，但我仍被存在的壮丽所贯穿，并比当僧侣时学到了更多真正的灵性。这并不意味着贫穷能通向真理，或苦难对灵魂有益。许多与我共享那些处境的人并没有学到什么。这确实意味着你们每个人都为自己选择那些生活境遇，事先知晓自己的弱点与长处何在。（停顿）
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In many ways this is a cozy concept, though to some it can be quite frightening with its connotations of eternal damnation. It is far too tidy an idea, however, to hint at the rich embellishments that are at the heart of divine creativity. The soul stands both within and without the fabric of physical life as you know it. You are not separated from the animals and the rest of existence by virtue of possessing an eternal inner consciousness. Such a consciousness is present within all living beings, and in all forms.
quotes.translation: 从许多方面来说，这是个舒适的概念，尽管对某些人而言，它带着永恒诅咒的意涵，可能相当令人恐惧。然而，这个想法过于整洁，无法暗示那位于神圣创造力核心的丰富装饰。灵魂既存在于你们所知的物质生命结构之内，也存在于其外。你们并非因拥有永恒的内在意识而与动物及其余存在分离。这种意识存在于所有生灵及一切形态之中。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You will understand, if you are a practicing idealist, that you cannot kill in the name of peace, for if you do so your methods will automatically undermine your ideal. The sacredness of life and spirit are one and the same. You cannot condemn the body without ultimately condemning the soul. You cannot condemn the soul without ultimately condemning the body.
quotes.translation: 如果你是个实践中的理想主义者，你会明白不能以和平之名杀戮，因为若如此做，你的方法会自动破坏你的理想。生命与灵性的神圣性是一体的。你无法在最终不谴责灵魂的情况下谴责身体，也无法在最终不谴责身体的情况下谴责灵魂。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

All four of us involved in the moving were quite amused, then -- for the friends who helped Jane and me carry the furniture yesterday are the father-and-son contracting team that built the room in the first place.

quotes.text: You have been trying to squeeze the soul into tight concepts of the nature of existence, making it follow your limited beliefs. The door to the soul is open, and it leads to all the dimensions of experience.
quotes.translation: 你们一直试图将灵魂挤进关于存在本质的狭隘概念中，让它追随你们有限的信念。灵魂之门是敞开的，它通向所有经验维度。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your beliefs have generated feelings of unworth. Having artificially separated yourselves from nature, you do not trust it, but often experience it as an adversary. Your religions granted man a soul, while denying any to other species. Your bodies then were relegated to nature and your souls to God, who stood immaculately apart from His creations.
quotes.translation: 你们的信念已生发出无价值感。你们人为地将自己与自然分离，不信任它，反而常将其视为对手。你们的宗教赋予人类灵魂，却否认其他物种拥有灵魂。于是你们的身体被归入自然，而灵魂被归入上帝——那位与祂的创造物完美分离的存在。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This talent for using energy to form unit patterns is elemental, not only on your plane but in all other planes. It involves drawing upon the basic vitality of the universe in using the inner senses, and actually pulling to oneself more and more of this under laying vitality. Lest this suggest images of grasp potbellied souls, gluttonously grabbing the stuff of the universe for themselves out of the mouths of the less ambitious, let me hasten to inform you that such is not the case.
quotes.translation: 这种运用能量形成单元模式的天赋是根本性的，不仅在你们层面如此，在所有其他层面亦然。它涉及运用内在感官汲取宇宙的基本活力，并实际上将越来越多这种底层活力拉向自身。唯恐这让人联想到大腹便便的灵魂，贪婪地从较不野心者口中抢夺宇宙物质，让我赶紧告知你：情况并非如此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: One prerequisite for such an intuitive understanding of the soul is the desire to achieve it. If the desire is strong enough, then you will be automatically led to experiences that will result in vivid, unmistakable subjective knowledge. There are methods that will enable you to do this, and I will give you some toward the end of this book.
quotes.translation: 对灵魂产生这种直觉性理解的前提之一，是渴望达成这种理解。若这份渴望足够强烈，你便会自然而然地被引领至那些能带来鲜活而确凿的主观认知的体验。确有方法能助你实现此目标，我将在本书末尾提供一些。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Using this as an analogy, you are a part of your psyche or your soul, dwelling within it, easily following your own sense of identity even though that psyche also contains other identities beside the one that you think of as your own. You draw sustenance from the world, and grow through its medium. You contribute your abilities and experience, helping to form the world's civilization and culture. To some strong degree you bear the same kind of relationship to your own psyche.
quotes.translation: 以此类比，你是自身心灵或灵魂的一部分，栖居其中，即便这心灵除了你视为己有的身份外还包含其他身份，你仍能轻易追随自己的身份感。你从世界中汲取滋养，通过其媒介成长。你贡献自身的能力与经验，协助塑造世界的文明与文化。在相当程度上，你与自身心灵的关系与此相似。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: He believed in Moses more than he did Allah, and I did not know until the last moment which form I was to assume. He was a very likable chap, and under the circumstances I did not mind when he seemed to expect a battle for his soul. Moses and Allah were to fight for him. He could not rid himself of the idea of force, though he had died by force, and nothing could persuade him to accept any kind of peace or contentment, or any rest, until some kind of battle was wrought.
quotes.translation: 他信仰摩西胜过信仰安拉，直到最后一刻我才知晓该以何种形象显现。他是个非常讨喜的家伙，在这种情况下，当他似乎期待一场为灵魂而战的争斗时，我并未介意。摩西与安拉要为他而战。他无法摆脱暴力的观念，尽管他正是死于暴力，除非某种争斗发生，否则没有任何事物能说服他接受任何形式的和平、满足或休憩。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This is the title for our book (Smile:) Seth Speaks: (colon) the Eternal Validity of the Soul. I am using the term soul, for it will have instant meaning to most readers. (Long pause.) I suggest you equip yourself with some good pens. Ask our friend Ruburt to kindly mail my outline, on my behalf, to our friend Aerofranz. (Smile.)
quotes.translation: 这是我们这本书的标题（微笑：）《赛斯如是说：（冒号）灵魂的永恒有效性》。我使用“灵魂”一词，因为它对多数读者而言具有即时意义。（长停顿。）我建议你备几支好笔。请我们的朋友鲁柏代我寄出大纲，给我们的朋友艾罗弗朗兹。（微笑。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Your body does not just happen to be thin or fat, tall or short, healthy or ill. These characteristics are mental, and are thrust outward by you upon your image. I do not mean to be facetious, but you were not born yesterday. Your soul was not born yesterday, in those terms, but before the annals of time as you think of time.
quotes.translation: 你的身体并非偶然地或胖或瘦、或高或矮、或健康或疾病。这些特质属于心智层面，由你向外投射于自身形象之上。我并非戏谑，但你并非昨日才诞生。就你所认知的时间而言，你的灵魂并非昨日才诞生，而是在时间纪年之前便已存在。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The soul can be considered as an electromagnetic energy field, of which you are part. It is a field of concentrated action when you consider it in this light -- a powerhouse of probabilities or probable actions, seeking to be expressed; a grouping of nonphysical consciousnesses that nevertheless knows itself as an identity. Look at it this way: The young woman through whom I speak once stated in a poem, and I quote: "These atoms speak, and call themselves my name."
quotes.translation: 灵魂可被视为一种电磁能量场，你便是其中一部分。若以此角度观之，它是浓缩行动的能量场——一个寻求表达的可能性或可能行动的能量库；一群非物质的意识体，却仍知晓自身为独立身份。不妨这样看：借我之口说话的年轻女子曾在一首诗中写道，我引述：“这些原子在言说，它们呼唤我的名字。”
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You are aware, alert, and participating in many more realities than you know as your soul expresses itself through you. That consciousness of your usual daylight hours, the ego consciousness, rises up like a flower from the ground of the "underneath," the unconscious bed of your own reality. Though you are not aware of it, this ego itself emerges, then falls back again into the unconscious, from which another ego then rises as a new bloom from the springtime earth.
quotes.translation: 你意识到、警觉着并参与着远比你所知的更多实相，因为你的灵魂正透过你表达自身。你日常清醒时刻的意识，即自我意识，如同花朵般从"底层"——你自身实相的无意识土壤——中升起。尽管你并未察觉，这个自我本身浮现而出，随后又落回无意识中，另一个自我便如春日大地绽放的新花般再度升起。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... The complete physician would be a person who learned to understand the dynamics of being, the soul-body relationship -- one who was healthy in his or her own body. Unhappy people cannot teach you to be happy. Sick ones cannot teach you to be well. Psychiatrists have a high suicide rate. Why do you think they can help you live happily, or add to your vitality? Physicians are not the healthiest of men by far.4 Why do you think they can cure you?
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……完整的医者应是学会理解存在动力学、灵魂-身体关系的人——一个自身身体康健的人。不快乐的人无法教你快乐。病弱之人无法教你健康。精神科医师的自杀率极高。你认为他们何以能助你快乐生活、增添你的生命力？医师远非最健康的人群。你认为他们何以能治愈你？
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: To other portions of yourself you would seem to be a sleepwalker. Full creative participation in any moment, however, awakens you to your own potentials, and therefore allows you to experience a unity between your own consciousness and the comprehension of your physical cells. Those cells are as spiritual as your soul is.
quotes.translation: 对你自身的其他部分而言，你仿佛是个梦游者。然而，全然创造性地参与任何当下，会唤醒你自身的潜能，从而让你体验到自身意识与物质细胞领悟力之间的统一。那些细胞与你的灵魂同样具有灵性。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 708
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Now, no psychological structure is easy to describe in words. Simply to explain the nature of personality as it is generally known, all kinds of terms are used: id, subconscious, ego, superego; all of these to differentiate the interweaving actions that make up the physical personality. The dreaming self is just as complicated. So you can say that certain portions of it deal with physical reality, physical manipulation, and plans; some with deeper levels of creativity and achievement that insure physical survival; some with communication, with even more extensive elements of the personality now generally unknown; some with the continuing experience and existence of what you may call the soul or overall individual entity, the true multidimensional self.
quotes.translation: 如今，任何心理结构都难以用言语描述。仅就一般所知的人格本质而言，人们便使用了各种术语：本我、潜意识、自我、超我——所有这些都用以区分构成物质人格的交织行动。做梦的自我同样复杂。因此你可以说，它的某些部分处理物质实相、物质操控与计划；某些部分涉及确保物质生存的更深层创造力与成就；某些部分处理沟通，涉及人格中通常未知的更广泛元素；某些部分处理你所谓灵魂或整体个体实体的持续经验与存在——那真正的多维自性。

quotes.text: You can feel what you cannot necessarily describe verbally, for you are more than the physical brain that you have now. I am no poet, but as in one of Ruburt's poems, think of the brain as a web you form about the inner self. This webwork helps you manipulate in a world of space and time, and is as nebulous, precarious and delicate as any spider's web -- and in as precarious a balance. You form this and then perceive the world, but your viewpoint is very small and the garden you perceive very intimate. You have, however, far greater abilities of perception. I want you to understand the nature of your inner self, or soul, for it is a focal point of reality from which other realities spring. It is not imprisoned in tiny boxes of days or weeks or months, or even of centuries.
quotes.translation: 你能感受到未必能用言语描述之物，因为你远胜于你此刻拥有的物质大脑。我并非诗人，但如鲁柏某首诗中所言：将大脑视为你围绕内在自性编织的网。这张网助你在时空世界中运作，它如任何蛛网般缥缈、脆弱且岌岌可危——处于同样危险的平衡中。你编织此网，继而感知世界，但你的视角极为狭小，你感知的花园极为私密。然而，你拥有远为强大的感知能力。我要你理解内在自性或灵魂的本质，因为它是实相的一个焦点，其他实相从中涌现。它并非囚禁于日、周、月甚至世纪的微小盒子里。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The point of all this is that these units are unpredictable, and fulfill all probabilities of consciousness. Any concepts of gods or other beings that are based upon limited ideas of personhood will ultimately be futile. You view the fantastic variety of physical life -- its animals, insects, birds, fish, man and all his works -- with hardly a qualm; yet you must understand that the nature of consciousness itself is far more varied, and you must learn to think of an inner reality that is as infinite as the exterior one. These concepts alone do alter your present consciousness, and change it in degree. The present idea of the soul, you see, is a "primitive" idea that can scarcely begin to explain the creativity or reality from which mankind's being comes. You are multipersons (intently). You exist in many times and places at once. You exist as one person, simultaneously. This does not deny the independence of the persons, but your inner reality straddles their reality, while it also serves as a psychic world in which they can grow.3
quotes.translation: 这一切的关键在于：这些单位不可预测，并实现了意识的所有可能性。任何基于有限人格观念的神祇或其他存有概念，最终都将徒劳无功。你目睹物质生命惊人的多样性——其动物、昆虫、鸟类、鱼类、人类及其所有造物——几乎毫无疑虑；然而你必须明白，意识本身的本质远为多样，你必须学会思考一个与外在实相同样无限的内在实相。仅这些概念就能改变你当前的意识，并使其发生程度上的转变。你看，当前关于灵魂的观念是一种"原始"观念，几乎无法开始解释人类存在所源自的创造力或实相。你们是多重人格（专注地）。你们同时存在于许多时空之中。你们同时作为一个人存在。这并不否认各个人的独立性，但你们的内在实相跨越了他们的实相，同时充当着他们得以在其中成长的心灵世界。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Animals have varying degrees of self-consciousness, as indeed people do. The consciousness that is within them is as valid and eternal as your own, however. There is nothing to prevent a personality from investing a portion of his own energy into an animal form. This is not transmigration of souls. It does not mean that a man can be reincarnated in an animal. It does mean that personalities can send a portion of their energy into various kinds of form.
quotes.translation: 动物拥有不同程度的自我意识，人类亦然。然而，它们内在的意识与你们的一样真实而永恒。人格完全可以将自身能量的一部分注入动物形态。这并非灵魂转世，不意味着人能转生为动物，而是指人格能将部分能量投入各种形态之中。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is not that I am holding back so much as that, in your terms, what follows is dependent upon an understanding of concepts presented earlier. People who are still worrying about one soul, gods, and devils, must be helped to relate to greater realities from their own framework, and gently led away from it if possible. Probabilities have been mentioned in such a way that alternate realities are presented, showing such people that choices are available.
quotes.translation: 并非我刻意保留信息，而是按你们的说法，后续内容取决于对先前概念的理解。那些仍在纠结单一灵魂、神祇与魔鬼的人，必须从自身框架出发去认知更宏大的实相，若有可能则温和地引导他们脱离原有认知。概率的提及方式展现了多重实相，让这些人看到选择的可能性。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: No psychological system is closed, no consciousness is closed, regardless of any appearances to the contrary within your own system. The soul is a traveler, as has been said so often; but it is also the creator of all experience, and of all destinations in your terms. It creates worlds as it goes, so to speak.
quotes.translation: 没有任何心理系统是封闭的，没有任何意识是封闭的，尽管你们系统内呈现相反的表象。灵魂是旅者——正如常被提及的那样——但按你们的说法，它也是所有经验与所有目的地的创造者。可以说，它一边前行，一边创造世界。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The soul is not only dressed in chemical clothes, but wears the apparel woven from all of the elements of the earth. As physical creatures you will be partially changed by any chemical or element, or food or drug that becomes part of your living system, but those effects will follow the nature of your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 灵魂不仅披着化学外衣，更穿着由大地所有元素编织的衣裳。作为物质生物，你们会因任何成为生命系统的化学物质、元素、食物或药物而部分改变，但这些效应将遵循你们信念的本质。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You felt before, unconsciously, that you were drifting and that life had little meaning. Beneath the surface of events you felt unfulfilled, and felt that you had great courage and abilities with never a chance to use them, and no "heroic" episodes then to rouse you to fuller understanding, and no real impetus to lift you or to bring excitement into your days. Unconsciously, therefore, you chose a situation in which a crisis was precipitated, rousing all the greatest elements of heart and soul, so that they must strain to understand, to perceive, to triumph. And so you shall, in whatever way is most important for you, and you will learn more and be more fulfilled than you would have been had those conditions not been initiated.
quotes.translation: 你们过去无意识地感到漂泊无依，生命缺乏意义。在事件表象之下，你们感到未得满足，觉得自己拥有巨大勇气与能力却永无用武之地，没有"英雄式"经历唤醒更深刻的领悟，没有真正动力提升你们或为日子注入激情。因此你们无意识地选择了引发危机的处境，唤醒心灵与灵魂中最伟大的元素，迫使它们竭力理解、感知、超越。你们终将以对自己最重要的方式达成目标，比起未曾触发这些条件的情况，你们将学到更多、获得更圆满的成就。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: (With much animation:) As an example, it appears to you that animals do not reflect upon their own reality. Certainly it seems that a cell has no "objective" knowledge of its own being, colon: as if it is without knowing what it is, or without appreciation of its own isness. You are quite wrong in such deductions. Nor are there necessarily gradations in which one kind of consciousness progresses in rigid terms from a lower to a higher state. Any cell has practical use of precognitive abilities,4 for example, that quite escape you, yet many of you assign such abilities to "higher" souls. Each kind of life has its own qualities that cannot be compared with those of others, and that often cannot be communicated.
quotes.translation: （生动地）举例来说，你们认为动物不会反思自身实相。细胞似乎对其存在没有"客观"认知——仿佛不知自己为何物，或无法体认自身的存在性。这种推论大错特错。意识也未必严格遵循从低级到高级的阶梯式进化。例如，任何细胞都实际运用着远超你们认知的预知能力，而你们许多人却将这种能力归于"高等"灵魂。每种生命形态都有其无法与其他形态比较的特质，这些特质往往无法被传达。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
