quotes.text: He saw it physically, yet could find no physical cause for it. It lasted several seconds and disappeared. As soon as Ruburt saw it he leaped back. The last line in the poem he had completed just before dinner spoke of a light that would illuminate both worlds, one of the soul and one of the flesh. Consciously he thought the light must have been caused by lightning, even while he knew with another portion of himself that that was not the case.
quotes.translation: 他确实看见了那道光，却找不到任何物理成因。它持续了几秒便消失了。鲁柏一看见它就往后跳。他晚餐前刚完成的诗作最后一行提到一道光，能照亮两个世界——灵魂的世界与肉体的世界。他意识上以为那光必是闪电所致，但内在的另一个自己却明白事实并非如此。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Christian dogma speaks of the ascension of Christ, implying of course a vertical ascent into the heavens, and the development of the soul is often discussed in terms of direction. To progress is supposedly to ascend, while the horror of religious punishment, hell, is seen at the bottom of all things.
quotes.translation: 基督教教义谈及基督升天，自然暗示着垂直升入天堂的意象，而灵魂的发展也常以方向性来讨论。进步被视为向上攀升，而宗教惩罚的恐怖——地狱——则被置于万物的底层。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In your terms, probabilities are extensions and variations on the growth principle that is quite obvious in your daily reality. Such growth is a natural manifestation flourishing within your particular area of actuality, observable to your senses. Again, other entirely natural manifestations of that principle exist. Some can only be glimpsed in distorted form because of other "natural" conditions that you cannot perceive. Probabilities involve you with a rich psychological growth and development, present but not observable in your "home ground." Any kind of existence happens within the context of nature, and nature includes the soul. Your definition of nature has simply been too limiting.
quotes.translation: 以你们的说法，概率是成长原则的延伸与变奏，这原则在你们的日常实相中显而易见。这种成长是自然的表现，在你们特定的实相领域里蓬勃发展，感官可察。同样，该原则还有其他完全自然的表现形式。有些只能以扭曲的形态被瞥见，因为你们无法感知其他"自然"条件。概率让你们涉入丰富的心理成长与发展，它存在于你们的"主场"却不可见。任何存在都发生在自然的脉络中，而自然包含灵魂。你们对自然的定义实在太狭隘了。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: I can say to you what you have not said to yourselves in your private moments. For as tables move so indeed do souls fly. Many in this room have abilities...my dear friend, the Jesuit, whose flippancy does not help him in his dark moments (to Bill) for you are not facing yourself and your abilities. This is the crux of the problem. It does you no good to smile. It is yourself with whom you must become friendly and yourself you must face.
quotes.translation: 我能说出你们在独处时未曾对自己说的话。因为正如桌子会移动，灵魂确实会飞翔。这房间里的许多人都有能力……我亲爱的朋友，那位耶稣会士（对比尔说），你的轻浮在黑暗时刻帮不了你——因为你没有面对自己和自己的能力。这是问题的关键。微笑对你无益。你必须与自己为友，必须面对自己。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: It is only because you have a highly limited conception of your own entity that you insist upon its being almost sterile in its singularity. There are millions of cells within your body, but you call your body a unit, and consider it your own. You do form it, from the inside out, and yet you form it from living substance, and each smallest particle has its own living consciousness. There are clumps of matter, and in that respect there are clumps of consciousness, each individual, with their own destiny and abilities and potentials. There are no limitations to your own entity: therefore, how can your entity or soul have boundaries, for boundaries would enclose it and deny it freedom.
quotes.translation: 只因你们对自己存有的概念极为局限，才坚持认为它在单一性中近乎贫瘠。你体内有数百万个细胞，但你称身体为一个单位，视之为己有。你确实从内而外地塑造它，却用活物质塑造它，每个最微小的粒子都有其活生生的意识。物质有团块，就此而言，意识也有团块——每个个体都有其命运、能力和潜能。你的存有没有限制：因此，你的存有或灵魂怎能有边界？因为边界会禁锢它，剥夺它的自由。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In a larger manner, the identity of the soul can be seen from the same viewpoint. It knows who it is, and is far more certain of its identity, indeed, than your physical self is of its identity. And yet now where in this electromagnetic energy field can the identity of the soul as such be found?
quotes.translation: 从更宏观的角度看，灵魂的身份也可用同样的观点来审视。它知道自己是谁，而且远比你的物质自我更确定自己的身份。然而，在这电磁能量场中，灵魂的身份本身又能在何处被找到呢？
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Not only is the entity strengthened, but portions of it, having been actualized in three-dimensional existence, now add to the very quality and nature of that existence. Without this creativity, planetary life in your terms would always be sterile. The soul or entity then gives breath to the body, and to the three-dimensional self within it. The three-dimensional self then goes about its purpose of opening up new areas of creativity.
quotes.translation: 不仅存有本身得到强化，而且那些已在三维存在中实现的部分，如今更增添了那种存在的特质与本质。若没有这种创造力，以你们的说法，行星生命将永远贫瘠。灵魂或存有于是赋予身体呼吸，也赋予其中的三维自我以生机。三维自我随后便致力于其目的：开拓创造力的新领域。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is now nearing Easter (on March 26), and the yearly commemoration of what is considered historic fact: the [resurrection and] ascension of Christ into heaven.1 Untold millions have in one way or another commemorated that occasion through the centuries. Private lives have merged with public sentiment and religious fervor. There have been numberless village festivals, or intimate family gatherings, and church services performed on Easter Sundays now forgotten. There have been bloody wars fought on the same account, and private persecutions in which those who did not agree with one or another's religious dogmas were quite simply killed "for the good of their souls."
quotes.translation: 如今临近复活节（3月26日），这是对被视为历史事实的纪念：基督升天。数个世纪以来，无数人以各种方式纪念这一时刻。私人生活与公众情感及宗教狂热融为一体。曾有无数乡村庆典、亲密家庭聚会，以及如今已被遗忘的复活节周日礼拜。同样为此爆发过血腥战争，也有私人迫害——那些不认同某种宗教教条的人，仅仅"为了他们灵魂的好处"而被杀害。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You may think of your soul or entity -- though only briefly and for the sake of this analogy -- as some conscious and living, divinely inspired computer who programs its own existences and lifetimes. But this computer is so highly endowed with creativity that each of the various personalities it programs spring into consciousness and song, and in turn create realities that may have been undreamed of by the computer itself.
quotes.translation: 你可以将你的灵魂或存有——尽管仅为类比而短暂如此设想——视为某种有意识、鲜活、受神启发的计算机，它编程自己的存在与生生世世。但这台计算机被赋予如此高度的创造力，以至于它所编程的每个不同人格都迸发出意识与歌声，进而创造出计算机自身可能从未梦想过的实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now: Often it seems that the soul is thought of as a precious stone, to be finally presented as a gift to God, or considered as some women used to consider their virginity -- something highly prized that must be lost; the losing of it being signified as a fine gift to the receiver.
quotes.translation: 现在：灵魂常被视为一颗宝石，最终要作为礼物献给上帝；或如同某些女性曾看待自己的贞操——某种珍贵之物，必须失去；而失去它则被视作对接受者的美好馈赠。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now, in the three-dimensional reality in which your ego has its main focus, becoming presupposes arrival, or a destination -- an ending to that which has been in a state of becoming. But the soul or entity has its existence basically in other dimensions, and in these, fulfillment is not dependent upon arrivals at any points, spiritual or otherwise.
quotes.translation: 现在，在你自我主要聚焦的三维实相中，"成为"预设了抵达或目的地——即对处于"成为"状态之物的终结。但灵魂或存有基本上存在于其他维度，在这些维度中，圆满并不依赖于抵达任何点——无论是灵性上的还是其他方面的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Since they do, of course, you can discover them now and learn to use them. This will directly (dreactly) assist you in after-death (agter) experience. You will not be nearly so startled by the nature of your own reactions (reactone) if you understand beforehand for example that your consciousness not only is not imprisoned by your physical body, but (bud) can create other portions at will. Those who "overidentify" their consciousness with their body can suffer self-created torment (tortment) for no reason, lingering about the body. Indeed, quite the forlorn soul, thinking it has no other place to go.
quotes.translation: 既然它们确实存在，你此刻就能发现它们并学会运用。这将直接帮助你在死后的体验。如果你事先理解，例如，你的意识不仅不受肉体禁锢，还能随意创造其他部分，那么你就不会对自己的反应本质感到如此震惊。那些将意识与身体"过度认同"的人，会无缘无故地遭受自我创造的折磨，在身体周围徘徊。确实，那正是凄凉的灵魂，以为自己无处可去。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: When most people think of reincarnation, they think in terms of a one-line progression in which the soul perfects itself in each succeeding life. This is a gross simplification. There are endless varieties of this one theme, individual variations. The process of reincarnation is used in many ways, therefore, and in this time of rest individuals must decide on the unique way in which reincarnation will be of use.
quotes.translation: 当大多数人想到转世时，他们想到的是灵魂在每一世中自我完善的线性进程。这是一种过于简化的说法。这个主题有无数种变体，因人而异。因此，转世的过程以多种方式被运用，在这段休息期间，个体必须决定转世将以何种独特的方式发挥作用。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I do not want you to think that the answers to your questions lie prepackaged in the dream state, either, relatively inaccessible except to those (long pause) who possess unique talents or some secretive knowledge of the world of the occult. Many people, long before the time of printing or reading, learned to read nature very well, to observe the seasons, to feel out "the seasons of the soul." The answers, therefore, lie as close as your own back-door steps, for at the thresholds of your beings you automatically stand in the center of knowledge. You are never at the periphery of events.
quotes.translation: 我也不希望你们认为问题的答案预先包装在梦境状态中，相对难以触及，只有那些（长停顿）拥有独特天赋或对神秘世界有秘密知识的人才能获得。早在印刷或阅读时代之前，许多人就学会了很好地解读自然，观察季节，感受"灵魂的季节"。因此，答案近在咫尺，就像你们自家后门的台阶，因为在你们存在的门槛上，你们自动站在知识的中心。你们从未处于事件的边缘。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Two and a half years ago I wrote in the closing notes for Session 869, in Chapter 10 of Mass Events, that she had finally received from Seth the full title for this book: Dreams, "Evolution," and Value Fulfillment. Mass Events was published eight months ago, and we have been receiving a steady flow of inquiries as to when Dreams will be issued. We're glad to try to answer questions like that, but our correspondents cover many topics and express many viewpoints--and some are hard for us to handle. As we waited for the session Jane gave me two very long letters she had received yesterday. I felt sad after reading them. We think that both writers express extreme points of view, and that both are much too adulatory of us. To answer one letter would draw its author to our doorstep at once: "I am your Seth," and: "I will visit you as soon as I hear from you." The writer of the other letter, while praising our work, is caught up in questions of conventional religion: "I keep wondering over and over again whether Seth is a demon or a deception. What do you have to say about this?" We don't feel like justifying ourselves.

quotes.text: Now: This discussion is not meant to be an esoteric presentation with little practical meaning in your daily lives. The fact is that while you hold limited concepts of your own reality, then you cannot practically take advantage of many abilities that are your own; and while you have a limited concept of the soul, then to some extent you cut yourself off from the source of your own being and creativity.
quotes.translation: 现在：这场讨论并非意在成为一场深奥的陈述，对你们的日常生活缺乏实际意义。事实上，当你们对自己实相持有有限的概念时，你们就无法实际利用许多属于你们自己的能力；而当你们对灵魂持有有限的概念时，你们就在某种程度上切断了自己与自身存在和创造力的源头。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: All of your physical experience must, of course, be pivoted in the corporeal reality of the body. The energy that moves your image comes from the soul. Through your own thoughts you direct the body's expression, and it can be of health or of illness. Out of a knowledge of the contents of your own conscious mind you can definitely heal most maladies of the body, within conditions to be given later.
quotes.translation: 当然，你们所有的物质体验都必须以身体的物质实相为轴心。驱动你们形象的能量来自灵魂。通过你们自己的思想，你们引导着身体的表达，它可以是健康或疾病。基于对自身意识心智内容的了解，你们确实可以治愈身体的大多数疾病，条件将在稍后给出。

quotes.text: Now: We do not need to name the two portions separately. I had thought of specifically having the first part deal with inner and outer immediate environments, and then to lead to the larger reality of the soul and its perception in the second part. This is what I did, but the material is so interwoven that I felt the given separation enough, and did not want to reinforce the idea of a division.
quotes.translation: 现在：我们不需要分别命名这两个部分。我曾考虑过让第一部分专门处理内在和外在的直接环境，然后引导到第二部分中灵魂的更广大实相及其感知。我确实这样做了，但材料如此交织在一起，以至于我觉得给出的划分已经足够，不想强化分裂的概念。

quotes.text: I wanted to go into the invention of the soul, using mankind's own terminology, the soul and the spirit being thought of as one and the same thing. The duality of which we spoke is mainly artificial. It is stronger in more so- called advanced societies.
quotes.translation: 我想探讨灵魂的发明，使用人类自己的术语，灵魂与灵被视为同一回事。我们所说的二元性主要是人为的。它在所谓更先进的社会中更为强烈。

quotes.text: Philosophies that teach denial of the flesh must ultimately end up preaching a denial of the self and building a contempt for it, because even though the soul is couched in muscle and bone it is meant to experience that reality, not to refute it.
quotes.translation: 教导否定肉身的哲学，最终必然宣扬否定自我并建立对自我的轻蔑，因为即使灵魂寄居于肌肉与骨骼之中，其本意也是体验那个实相，而非否定它。

quotes.text: I mention this again in the middle of our present discussion so that you do not feel lost, or negated, or insignificant. There are obviously an infinite number of gradations in the types and kinds of forms of which we have been speaking. That energy which is projected from our "superself," that spark of intense identity that resulted in your physical birth, that unique impetus, in the one way has many similarities to the old concept of the soul -- except that it contains only a part of the story.
quotes.translation: 在我们当前讨论的中途再次提及这点，是为了让你不会感到迷失、被否定或微不足道。显然，我们一直在讨论的形态类型与种类存在无限多的层次。那股从"超我"投射出的能量，那股导致你肉身诞生的强烈身份火花，那种独特的推动力，在某种程度上与古老的灵魂概念有许多相似之处——只是它只包含了故事的一部分。

quotes.translation: 疾病与痛苦并非由神、或一切万有、或任何外在力量强加于你。它们是学习过程的副产品，由你自身创造，本身相当中性……疾病与痛苦是创造性能量被误导的结果。然而，它们仍是创造力的一部分。它们的来源与健康活力并无不同。痛苦对灵魂并无益处，除非它教导你如何停止受苦——那才是它的目的。

It grows and develops even through the experience of those personalities that compose it, and it is, to put it as simply as possible, more than the sum of its parts.
quotes.translation: 然而，存在一种内在的沟通，任何个体都能获得其中一个的知识——并非在肉体的死亡之后，而是在你当下的此刻。如前所述，灵魂本身并非静止不变。它甚至通过构成它的那些人格的经验而成长和发展，并且，简单来说，它大于其各部分的总和。

quotes.translation: 灵魂幻想，或灵性幻想，大约在此时兴起，并对他造成了不利，因为它给整体自性的某一部分赋予了名称和标签，将其与另一部分对立起来。然而，正是这个基本概念，迫使他不得不面对一个真相——即持续存在的真相，他称之为不朽。

quotes.text: "God knows itself through the flesh. God may know itself through a million or a thousand million other worlds, as so may I -- but because this world is, and because I am alive in it, it is more than appearance, more than a shackle to be thrown aside. It is a privilege to be here, to look out with this unique focus, with these individual eyes; not to be blinded by cosmic vision, but to see this corner of reality which I form through the miraculous connections of soul and flesh.
quotes.translation: "神通过肉体认识自身。神可能通过百万或亿万其他世界认识自身，正如我也可能如此——但因为这个世界的存在，因为我活在其中，它便超越了表象，超越了将被抛弃的桎梏。能在此处，能以这独特的焦点、以这双独特的眼睛向外观看，是一种特权；不被宇宙视野所蒙蔽，而是看见这个我通过灵魂与肉体的神奇连接所形成的现实角落。

quotes.text: The greater sweep of the seasons represents the reaches of your soul. You will not attain spirituality by turning your eyes away from nature, or by trying to disentangle yourself from it. You will not "glimpse eternal life" by attempting to deny the life that you have now -- for that life is your own unique path, and provides its own clues for you to follow.
quotes.translation: 季节的更迭代表了你们灵魂的延伸。你们不会通过将目光从自然移开，或试图脱离自然而获得灵性。你们不会通过否认你们现在所拥有的生命而'瞥见永恒的生命'——因为那个生命是你们自己独特的道路，并提供了你们可以遵循的线索。

quotes.text: They represent a present unique beyond telling, alive in each consciousness, more important than you recognize. There are no real rules to be followed that will bring you into such an encounter with the present moment of reality -- only a trust in the nature of your being. And that trust is within you whether or not you recognize it, for it gives you your present experience; and no matter how your mind questions, it rides securely in the great creativity of the soul.
quotes.translation: 它们代表了一种无法言喻的独特当下，活跃于每一个意识中，比你们所认知的更为重要。没有真正的规则可以遵循，能让你们与现实的当下时刻相遇——只有对你们存在本质的信任。而那份信任就在你们之内，无论你们是否认识到它，因为它赋予了你们当下的经验；无论你们的心智如何质疑，它都安然地乘着灵魂的伟大创造力前行。

quotes.text: Your consciousness then takes many forms, and these forms need not be alike any more, say, than a caterpillar is like a butterfly. The soul or entity has complete freedom of expression. It changes its form to suit its expression, and it forms environments like stage settings, and worlds to suit its purposes. Each setting brings forth new developments.
quotes.translation: 你的意识随后会采取许多形式，而这些形式不必彼此相似，就像毛毛虫与蝴蝶不必相似一样。灵魂或存有拥有完全的表达自由。它改变自己的形态以适应其表达，并像舞台布景一样构建环境，创造世界以符合其目的。每个场景都会带来新的发展。

quotes.text: Moreover, there is nothing to stop it from exploring this other greater identity, or moving into it, so to speak. When this happens both identities are changed. In greater terms, the psyche or soul nowhere exists as a finished product or entity. On the other hand it is always becoming, and that becoming happens on the part of each of its own portions.
quotes.translation: 此外，没有任何事物能阻止它探索这个更伟大的身份，或可以说，进入其中。当这种情况发生时，两个身份都会改变。从更宏大的角度来看，心灵或灵魂在任何地方都不以成品或存有的形式存在。相反，它始终在生成中，而这种生成发生在它自身的每一个部分上。

quotes.text: A friend and I, with some others, staged the ceremony, and from opposite clouds in the sky Allah and I shouted out our claims upon his soul -- while he, poor man, cowered on the ground between us. Now while I tell this story humorously, you must understand that the man's belief brought it about, and so to set him free, we worked it through.
quotes.translation: 我和一位朋友，连同其他一些人，主持了这场仪式，从天空相对的云层中，安拉和我大声宣称我们对他的灵魂拥有主权——而他，可怜的人，蜷缩在我们之间的地面上。现在我幽默地讲述这个故事时，你必须明白，是那个人的信念导致了这一切，因此为了让他解脱，我们通过这种方式解决了问题。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: It is only because your present existence is so highly focused in one narrow area that you put such stern limits upon your definitions and the self, and then project these upon your concepts of the soul. You worry for your physical identity and limit the extent of your perceptions for fear you cannot handle more and retain your selfhood.
quotes.translation: 仅仅因为你们当前的存在如此高度聚焦于一个狭窄的领域，你们才对自己的定义和自我施加如此严格的限制，然后将这些限制投射到你们对灵魂的概念上。你们为物质身份担忧，并限制感知的范围，因为害怕无法处理更多而仍保持自我。

quotes.text: A scientist examining nature studies its exterior, observing the outsideness of nature. Even investigative work involving atoms and molecules, or [theoretical2] faster-than-light particles, concerns the particle nature of reality. The scientist does not usually look for nature's heart. He certainly does not pursue the study of its soul.
quotes.translation: 一位科学家在审视自然时研究其外表，观察自然的外在性。即使涉及原子和分子，或超光速粒子的研究工作，也关注实相的粒子性质。科学家通常不寻找自然的核心。他当然不会追求研究自然的灵魂。

quotes.translation: 许多事情尚不为人所知。你们的心理学家无法以灵魂的术语思考，而你们的宗教领袖无法或拒绝从心理学角度理解它，哪怕是最简单的程度。换句话说，形而上学和心理学尚未相遇。

quotes.text: You do have access to the inner self, therefore. You are hardly cut off from your own soul or entity. The ego prefers to consider itself the captain at the helm, so to speak, since it is the ego who most directly deals with the sometimes tumultuous seas of physical reality, and it does not want to be distracted from this task.
quotes.translation: 因此，你确实能够接触内在自己。你并未与自己的灵魂或存有隔绝。自我更倾向于将自己视为掌舵的船长，因为它直接应对物理实相中时而汹涌的波涛，不愿被这项任务分心。

quotes.text: One of the advantages of the inner senses as they operate together is this ability to experience whole entirety patterns, and it is difficult to dilute this, to drop it so to speak, drop by drop. I would like to say something about Joseph's experience the other evening, but I also want to go into the invention of the soul.
quotes.translation: 内在感官协同运作时的一大优势，便是能体验完整的整体模式，而要将这种体验稀释成点滴片段实属困难。我想谈谈约瑟夫前晚的经历，但同时也想深入探讨灵魂的发明。

quotes.text: The reality of dreams themselves can only be investigated through direct contact. Their reality cannot be probed by scientific devices, for dreams in this respect are as nebulous as the spirit, or soul, or inner self. Dreams are directly experienced. They have no meaning outside of their relationship with the personality.
quotes.translation: 梦境本身的实相只能通过直接接触来探究。科学仪器无法探测其真实性，因为在这方面，梦境与灵、灵魂或内在自己一样难以捉摸。梦境是直接被体验的，它们与人格的关系之外毫无意义。

quotes.text: It continues to grow and develop, but its growth and development is highly dependent upon its realization that while it is distinct and individual, it is also but one manifestation of the soul. To the extent that it realizes this, it learns to unfold in creativity, and to use those abilities that lie inherent within it.
quotes.translation: 它持续成长发展，但其成长高度依赖于一个认知：虽然它独特而个体化，却也只是灵魂的一种显现。当它意识到这一点，便学会在创造力中展开，并运用自身固有的能力。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The soul perceives all experience directly. Most experiences of which you are aware come packaged in physical wrapping, and you take the wrapping for the experience itself, and do not think of looking inside. The world that you know is one of the infinite materializations taken by consciousness, and as such it is valid.
quotes.translation: 灵魂直接感知所有经验。你所觉察的大多数经验都包裹在物质外衣中，你将外衣误认为经验本身，却从未思考其内在。你所知的世界是意识无数具体化形式之一，因此它同样真实有效。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now, I have given you what you wanted, and some information that I thought you needed, and I will end the session: like Old King Cole, a merry old soul was he. (Smile, emphatic.)
quotes.translation: 现在，我已给予你所求之物，以及一些我认为你需要的资讯，我将结束此节：就像老国王科尔，他是个快乐的老灵魂。（微笑，强调语气。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Your idea of one soul, one self, forms a significance and a selectivity that blinds you to these other realities that are as much "here and now" as your present self. The units of consciousness that compose your physical being alone are aware of those greater significances, to which your limited ideas make you opaque.
quotes.translation: 你们对单一灵魂、单一自我的观念，形成了一种意义与选择性，使你们对同样存在于"此时此地"的其他实相视而不见——就像你们现在的自我一样。构成你们物质实相的意識单位本身，就知晓那些更宏大的意义，而你们受限的观念使你们对其浑然不觉。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 683
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: When you let your attention waver, however, you may often fall into a state in which you momentarily perceive glimpses of another alternate present. The whole self, the soul, knows of its reality in all such systems, and you, as a part of it, are working toward the same state of self-awareness and development.
quotes.translation: 然而，当你们让注意力游移时，常常会陷入一种状态，在其中瞬间瞥见另一个交替的当下。整体自我、灵魂，知晓自身在所有这类系统中的实相，而你们作为它的一部分，正朝着同样的自我觉知与发展状态迈进。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Illness and suffering are the results of the misdirection of creative energy. They are a part of the creative force, however. They do not come from a different source than, say, health and vitality. Suffering is not good for the soul, unless it teaches you how to stop suffering. That is its purpose.
quotes.translation: 疾病与痛苦是创造力被误导的结果。但它们仍是创造力的一部分，并非来自与健康活力不同的源头。痛苦对灵魂并无益处，除非它教会你如何停止受苦——那才是它的目的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now: All of this may seem to have little to do with the nature of reincarnation, as you think of it, or with counterparts as I have explained them. Yet it is vital that you throw aside old concepts of the self and of the soul before you can begin to understand the freedom of your own selfhood.
quotes.translation: 现在：这一切似乎与你们所理解的转世本质，或与我解释过的对应人物关系不大。但至关重要的是，在你们开始理解自身自我性的自由之前，必须先抛弃关于自我和灵魂的旧有概念。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now, the soul is never diminished, nor basically are any portions of the self.
quotes.translation: 灵魂永不减损，自我的任何部分也基本如此。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: There are many matters here very difficult to express in words, for you are so afraid for your sense of identity that you resist the idea that the soul, for example, is an open spiritual system, a powerhouse of creativity that shoots out in all directions -- and yet this is indeed the case.
quotes.translation: 有许多事情极难用言语表达，因为你们如此恐惧失去身份认同感，以至于抗拒这样的观念：例如，灵魂是一个开放的精神系统，一座向四面八方迸发创造力的发电厂——然而事实确实如此。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is almost impossible to begin with concepts of one isolated universe, one self at the mercy of its past, one time sequence, and end up with any acceptable theory of a multidimensional soul or godhead that is anything else but a glorified personified concept of what you think man is.6
quotes.translation: 几乎不可能从孤立宇宙、受制于过去的自我、单一时间序列的概念出发，最终得出任何可接受的多维灵魂或神性理论——除非那不过是人类自认为的、被神格化的自我概念。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Now you may call this a spiritual or psychological or psychic exploration, as you prefer. You will not be trying to find your soul. In that respect there is nothing to find. It is not lost, and you are not lost. The words you use may make no difference, but your intent does indeed.
quotes.translation: 现在你可以称此为灵性探索、心理探索或心灵探索，随你喜好。你并非在寻找你的灵魂。就这方面而言，根本没有什么可寻找的。它并未迷失，你也未曾迷失。你使用的词语或许无关紧要，但你的意图确实至关重要。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: That soul constantly creates the body, and each individual on the face of the earth at any given time places his or her trust in that reality. That feeling of certainty is the same that any plant knows. Any idea, creative insight, or dream, rides upon the same sure thrust.
quotes.translation: 那个灵魂持续创造着身体，地球上每个个体在任何特定时刻都将信任寄托于这实相。这种确定感与任何植物所知的感受相同。任何想法、创意洞见或梦境，都乘着同样笃定的推力前行。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (Very slowly at 11:18:) The conscious mind is an excellent perceiving attribute, a function that belongs to inner awareness but in this case is turned outward toward the world of events. Through the conscious mind the soul looks outward. Left alone, it perceives clearly.
quotes.translation: （11:18分极缓慢地：）意识心是卓越的感知属性，属于内在觉知的功能，但在此情况下转向外在的事件世界。通过意识心，灵魂向外观看。当它不受干扰时，便能清晰感知。

quotes.text: If on the other hand you carry the idea too far -- that illness can also be a learning process -- then you can fall into the other extreme, glorifying sickness or disease as a necessary ennobling experience in which the body is purged so that the soul can be saved.
quotes.translation: 反之，若你将"疾病也可以是学习过程"的观念推至极端，便会落入另一极端——将病痛美化为必要的崇高体验，认为身体需经此净化，灵魂方能得救。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Now those who have such beliefs actually lack a necessary deep trust in the nature of consciousness, of the soul, and of All That Is. They concentrate upon not what they think of as the power of good, but fearfully upon what they think of as the power of evil.
quotes.translation: 那些持有此类信念的人，实际上缺乏对意识本质、灵魂本质及一切万有本质的必要深层信任。他们并非专注于所谓的善之力量，而是恐惧地聚焦于所谓的恶之力量。

quotes.text: Now I have often said that suffering of itself is not "good for the soul." It is not a virtue, yet certainly many individuals seem to seek suffering. Suffering cannot be dismissed from human experience as a freak matter of distorted emotions or beliefs.
quotes.translation: 我常说过，痛苦本身并非"对灵魂有益"。它不是一种美德，然而确实有许多人似乎在寻求痛苦。痛苦不能被视为扭曲情绪或信念的异常现象而从人类经验中排除。

quotes.text: The "Unknown" Reality, Volumes One and Two. Seth reveals the multidimensional nature of the human soul, the dazzling labyrinths of unseen probabilities involved in any decision, and how probable realities combine to create the waking life we know.
quotes.translation: 《未知的实相》卷一、卷二。赛斯揭示了人类灵魂的多维本质、任何决定中涉及的无形概率的炫目迷宫，以及概率性实相如何组合成我们所知的清醒生活。

quotes.text: At one level of your being there is a common ground where body consciousness merges with that higher consciousness from which your own identity springs. This is the ground of your being where soul and flesh meet, both in time and out of it.
quotes.translation: 在你存在的某个层面，存在一个共同基础，身体意识在此与源自你自身身份的那个更高意识融合。这是你存在的根基，灵魂与肉体在此相遇，既在时间之内，也在时间之外。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: To a certain extent what you are was latent in the fetus, but there is no one point when "the full awareness of the soul enters into the flesh." The process is gradual. In physical terms it begins before your own parents are born.
quotes.translation: 在某种程度上，你之所是早已潜在于胎儿之中，但不存在"灵魂的完整觉知进入肉体"的某个特定时刻。这个过程是渐进的。就物质层面而言，它始于你父母出生之前。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
